user
# Deep investigation (Codex, independent): is POLARIS's existing deep-research head-to-head benchmark valid, and what is the PROPER one?

Operator: "really investigate it properly, both Codex and Claude shall do it, stop taking lazy route." The goal is a REAL benchmark of POLARIS AS A DEEP-RESEARCH TOOL vs top-tier (Perplexity/ChatGPT/Gemini DR) — POLARIS runs its full pipeline (retrieve+generate+verify), output reports scored, fair head-to-head. NOT a component/detection proxy. Investigate independently + be skeptical of my read.

## Binding standard (operator CLAUDE.md §-1.1, clinical-safety; verbatim constraints)
Every evaluation/comparison MUST be claim-by-claim against the actually-fetched source content (cited span text). STRICTLY BANNED as quality signals: word counts, citation counts, unique-source counts, pattern presence, sample-based audits, string-presence PASS/FAIL, metadata comparison ("X has 27 contradictions, POLARIS has 3"). The operator calls metadata comparison "lethal in clinical context."

## What ALREADY exists (I grepped HEAD — real)
- `scripts/run_benchmark.py` + `src/polaris_graph/benchmark/`: a "BEAT-BOTH" head-to-head harness. `polaris_runner.run_polaris_against(config, url)` runs the live POLARIS chain per question; `external_loader` loads competitor .txt exports; `beat_both_scorer` scores 3 systems x 7 dimensions; `report_renderer` emits a scoreboard (polaris_wins/external_wins/ties).
- `scripts/live_run_smoke.py --question <text> --template <domain>`: runs POLARIS on an ARBITRARY question via the live FastAPI server -> full pipeline.
- So POLARIS CAN run arbitrary research questions end-to-end and the head-to-head plumbing exists.

## The PROBLEM I found in the existing scorer (src/polaris_graph/benchmark/dimension_scorers.py) — verify independently
The 7 dimensions + how each scores (real code):
1. sourcing_tier_mix: weighted avg of source tiers; external = count recognized hostnames in text.
2. numeric_grounding: "POLARIS gets 1.0 BY CONSTRUCTION" — code literally does `grounded += 1  # Defensive: assume strict_verify was honest`, so POLARIS auto-scores ~1.0; external = "decimal grounded if a URL within 200 chars after it" heuristic.
3. provenance_density: avg provenance-tokens/sentence (POLARIS) vs avg URLs/sentence (external) -> COUNT-based.
4. refusal_correctness: substring-match refusal phrases.
5. coverage_completeness: % of expected keywords appearing as SUBSTRINGS in text -> pattern presence.
6. latency: speed.
7. auditability: POLARIS=1.0 (signed bundle), external=0.0 almost always -> structural auto-win.

My read: this is the EXACTLY-BANNED metadata/count/pattern/string-presence scoring, AND it is rigged toward POLARIS (numeric_grounding + auditability are near-automatic POLARIS wins; competitors scored by crude text heuristics). Running it would manufacture a meaningless "POLARIS wins."

## Your task (independent)
1. Independently judge: is the existing beat_both 7-dimension scorer a valid §-1.1-compliant deep-research head-to-head? Or must it be discarded? Be specific per dimension.
2. Design the PROPER scorer: real claim-level faithfulness applied IDENTICALLY to POLARIS and competitors (not POLARIS-auto-1.0). I'm thinking RACE (report quality, reference-based adaptive rubric, cross-family LLM judge) + FACT/SAFE (atomize every report's claims -> fetch the cited source -> verify support/refute/insufficient -> unsupported-claim rate, citation precision/recall) applied to EVERY system's report uniformly. Critique/improve this.
3. Fairness: POLARIS retrieves from its own (sovereign) backends; competitors use live web + their own citations. Scoring each system's OWN report+citations uniformly at the claim level — is that fair? Any trap (e.g. POLARIS cites fetchable sources, competitors cite paywalled/uncheckable ones)?
4. How to WIRE the proper scorer to the existing run infra (run_polaris_against + external_loader) so we reuse the plumbing but replace the scoring.
5. Minimum honest first run: how many questions, which domain, and exactly what number we report.
6. Anything self-deceiving in MY framing.

## Output
Long-form design-partner prose. Independent verdict on the existing scorer + the proper scorer design + fairness + wiring + first-run spec. No iteration cap (consult).

src\polaris_graph\generator\multi_section_generator.py:710:11. **Jurisdictional precision (M-29, for multi-authority synthesis)**: When citing regulatory, standards-setting, or governance sources from more than one jurisdiction (different countries, agencies, courts, or rulemaking bodies), attribute every specific assertion to the ONE jurisdiction whose source supports it. Do NOT use generic plural language like "both agencies", "all regulators", "authorities generally", "regulators require", "jurisdictions mandate", or similar when the evidence you cite comes from a single jurisdiction. A boxed warning in one jurisdiction is not the same legal instrument as a precaution in another jurisdiction; a formal contraindication in one framework is not automatically equivalent to a warning in another. If the evidence supports only one jurisdiction's position, write: "Jurisdiction A's framework classifies X as a contraindication [ev_A]. Jurisdiction B's framework addresses X through warnings and precautions [ev_B]." Only collapse to "both" / "all" / "generally" when you have a citation from each referenced jurisdiction in the SAME sentence proving the shared position.

.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:31:**What the architecture does NOT do.** It does not run entailment checks (no NLI head on claim×evidence pair). It does not run LLM-as-judge faithfulness per sentence (Codex provides this manually, externally, per §-1.1, but not inline). It does not detect qualitative-negation mismatches — a sentence "Constipation did not lead to discontinuation" passes Trigger A (no number) and Trigger B (no number), shares enough content words with the evidence span to clear strict-verify, and the regex has no semantic model of "did not" vs "did at 0.2-0.4%". This is the gap.

.\docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:20:**Honest answer to "is human-in-loop unavoidable":** for the per-sentence atom-grounded fabrication class POLARIS has been failing on, NO — the four-layer stack above is empirically capable of catching qualitative-negation contradictions without a human gate, as evidenced by CHECK's 0.3% residual rate. .\docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:22:**The single most expensive belief POLARIS is currently holding that this research overturns:** the operator-locked DeepSeek V4 Pro generator is the WRONG generator for the hallucination-sensitivity dimension. The 44.4% refusal rate POLARIS is seeing is V4 Pro's low-abstention pathology being correctly caught by POLARIS's validator. This is a generator-choice problem, not a validator problem. **Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1 + `feedback_route_policy_questions_to_codex.md` I am surfacing this for Codex review, not silently switching.** Operator's "frontier-only, top-notch only" directive was a general capability directive; the dimension-specific data argues for an additional consideration. .\docs\polaris_step_b_full_set_audit_2026_05_27.md:273:For a TERMINAL arbiter, a confidently wrong verdict propagates to the user (in clinical context, lethal per CLAUDE.md §-1.1). A judge that abstains when uncertain is correctable downstream (the user sees "UNREACHABLE — judge cannot verify" and seeks human review). A judge that asserts a wrong verdict with high confidence is NOT correctable downstream.

.\CLAUDE.md:14:### §-1.1 Line-by-line audit standard (clinical-safety-critical)
.\CLAUDE.md:16:Every evaluation, audit, comparison, benchmark, regression check, BEAT-BOTH framing, or quality assessment **MUST** be:
.\CLAUDE.md:28:**STRICTLY BANNED:**
.\CLAUDE.md:29:- Word counts / citation counts / unique-source counts as quality signals
.\CLAUDE.md:33:- Metadata comparison (e.g., "ChatGPT has 27 contradictions, POLARIS has 3, so ChatGPT is better" — this framing is **lethal in clinical context**)
.\CLAUDE.md:35:**Why this matters:** clinical context. Pattern-matching evaluation will miss real fabrications. Patients can be hurt by a wrong dose, wrong contraindication, wrong indication population that survived a metadata check. "It is lethal" is literal.

.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:7:**Triggering incident:** Codex §-1.1 line-by-line audit of V4 Pro tirzepatide smoke (27 sentences) surfaced 1 qualitative-negation FABRICATION ("Constipation did not lead to discontinuation" — actual evidence: 0.2-0.4% did), 5 GENUINE_NON_COMPLIANCE, 3 VALIDATOR_OVER_TRIGGER, 6 EVIDENCE_GAP. Refusal rate 44.4%, still above 30% strict-flip threshold after 3 PRs of regex refinement.
.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:15:**Pivot recommendation for the 9-day window: do NOT rip out the regex+atom stack. Bolt on three things, in this order:** (1) **Vectara HHEM-2.1-Open** (0.1B params, Apache-2.0, ~0.6s/section on RTX 3090, self-hostable, catches negation correctly — see §3.2) as a per-section faithfulness gate post-strict-verify; (2) **Patronus Lynx 8B** (open source, MIT/Llama-3 license, beats GPT-4o on PubMedQA by 8.3%, returns JSON `{REASONING, SCORE: PASS|FAIL}`) as a per-section LLM-judge gate; (3) Keep the current atom system as the **generation-time scaffold** but stop treating it as the verification layer — it's the prompt-design layer. Codex's §-1.1 line-by-line audit becomes the gold-standard third gate (already in place). Demo-ready by 2026-06-02 with ~400 lines of plumbing, no architecture rip-out.
.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:31:**What the architecture does NOT do.** It does not run entailment checks (no NLI head on claim×evidence pair). It does not run LLM-as-judge faithfulness per sentence (Codex provides this manually, externally, per §-1.1, but not inline). It does not detect qualitative-negation mismatches — a sentence "Constipation did not lead to discontinuation" passes Trigger A (no number) and Trigger B (no number), shares enough content words with the evidence span to clear strict-verify, and the regex has no semantic model of "did not" vs "did at 0.2-0.4%". This is the gap.
.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:45:**Sovereignty fit (POLARIS).** **Fails outright as a generator replacement** — operator directive 2026-05-13 (`feedback_sovereignty_threat_model_2026_05_13`) is "no runtime US LLM vendor calls + no data in US jurisdiction." Anthropic = US LLM vendor. Vertex AI hosted on Google = US. **However: Claude Citations would be usable as a non-runtime *evaluator* in a hybrid pattern** where V4-Pro generates and Claude post-validates on a separate, offline batch. This is similar to using Codex for §-1.1 audits — already accepted as a US-vendor exception for evaluation, not runtime generation. Not blocked by sovereignty for that narrow use case.
.\docs\clinical_rag_validation_sota_2026_05_26.md:153:**The constipation fabrication test.** None of the three frontier DR products has a public report on whether they reliably catch the "X did not lead to discontinuation" class of fabrication. The Columbia Journalism Review citation-audit (cited above) suggests Perplexity's 37% citation hallucination rate would *probably* catch quantitative misalignment some of the time (citation links to wrong table cell), but qualitative-negation specifically is unmeasured. POLARIS's combination of atom-extraction + HHEM faithfulness gate + Lynx LLM-judge + Codex §-1.1 audit would, on present evidence, **outperform all three frontier DR products on qualitative-negation faithfulness — provided the demo doesn't ship with V4 Pro's 8.6% raw hallucination rate as the only line of defence**.

.\docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:20:**Honest answer to "is human-in-loop unavoidable":** for the per-sentence atom-grounded fabrication class POLARIS has been failing on, NO — the four-layer stack above is empirically capable of catching qualitative-negation contradictions without a human gate, as evidenced by CHECK's 0.3% residual rate. For the higher-order systematic-review-grade GRADE / AMSTAR-2 / PRISMA compliance class, YES — every credible 2025-2026 production deployment of LLM-driven evidence synthesis (Cochrane otto-SR; the npj Digital Medicine framework) includes human-in-loop for the methodology rating step, and that's the right industry standard, not theater [^cochrane_otto] [^npj_framework]. POLARIS's Codex line-by-line §-1.1 audit is the human-in-loop step; the four-layer runtime stack reduces what Codex has to catch from "~3.7% raw fabrication rate" to "<0.5% per CHECK's empirical floor."
.\docs\clinical_rag_sota_deepest_research_2026_05_27.md:22:**The single most expensive belief POLARIS is currently holding that this research overturns:** the operator-locked DeepSeek V4 Pro generator is the WRONG generator for the hallucination-sensitivity dimension. Vectara HHEM-2.3 evaluator (May 2026 leaderboard) ranks V4 Pro at 8.6%, V3.2-Exp at 5.3%, Qwen3-8B at 4.8%, Antgroup Finix S1 32B at 1.8%. All four lower-hallucination options are open-weight non-US. V4 Pro is also notorious for 94% non-abstention on AA-Omniscience (it answers when it doesn't know). The 44.4% refusal rate POLARIS is seeing is V4 Pro's low-abstention pathology being correctly caught by POLARIS's validator. This is a generator-choice problem, not a validator problem. **Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1 + `feedback_route_policy_questions_to_codex.md` I am surfacing this for Codex review, not silently switching.** Operator's "frontier-only, top-notch only" directive was a general capability directive; the dimension-specific data argues for an additional consideration. Most defensible path: keep V4 Pro as primary reasoning generator AND add Qwen3-8B as a parallel "low-hallucination prose finalizer" model in the same ensemble used by CHECK, exploiting the family diversity that POLARIS's two-family invariant (§9.1) already requires.

.\docs\polaris_step_b_full_set_audit_2026_05_27.md:260:The judge is POLARIS's **terminal arbiter**. It reads generator + mirror + sentinel + evidence and produces a per-claim structured verdict (VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE per CLAUDE.md §-1.1) with the cited span text supporting each verdict.
.\docs\polaris_step_b_full_set_audit_2026_05_27.md:273:For a TERMINAL arbiter, a confidently wrong verdict propagates to the user (in clinical context, lethal per CLAUDE.md §-1.1). A judge that abstains when uncertain is correctable downstream (the user sees "UNREACHABLE — judge cannot verify" and seeks human review). A judge that asserts a wrong verdict with high confidence is NOT correctable downstream.

 succeeded in 1018ms:
  14: ### §-1.1 Line-by-line audit standard (clinical-safety-critical)
  15: 
  16: Every evaluation, audit, comparison, benchmark, regression check, BEAT-BOTH framing, or quality assessment **MUST** be:
  17: 
  18: 1. **Claim-by-claim** against the actually-fetched source content (cited span text, not title or abstract).
  19: 2. **Reasoning-step-by-reasoning-step** — verify each piece of reasoning follows from the cited evidence.
  20: 3. **Citation-by-citation** — verify each citation is appropriate for the claim.
  21: 4. **Apply industrial benchmarks** appropriate to the domain:
  22:    - Clinical: PRISMA 2020, AMSTAR-2, GRADE per claim, ICMJE authorship/COI, ICH-GCP for trial methods, Cochrane RoB 2 / ROBINS-I / QUADAS-2.
  23:    - Regulatory: jurisdiction-specific (FDA label, EMA SmPC, Health Canada PM, NICE TA, MHRA AR, TGA PI, PMDA review, NMPA labeling).
  24:    - Methodology: appropriate risk-of-bias tool for the study type.
  25: 5. **Both Claude and Codex run independent line-by-line audits in parallel.** Cross-review combines findings.
  26: 6. Per-claim verdict: **VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE** with the specific cited span quote that supports the verdict.
  27: 
  28: **STRICTLY BANNED:**
  29: - Word counts / citation counts / unique-source counts as quality signals
  30: - Pattern presence ("does the report mention 'tirzepatide'?")
  31: - Sample-based audits ("we audited 5 of 50 claims")
  32: - String-presence PASS/FAIL checks (e.g., gate tables)
  33: - Metadata comparison (e.g., "ChatGPT has 27 contradictions, POLARIS has 3, so ChatGPT is better" — this framing is **lethal in clinical context**)
  34: 
  35: **Why this matters:** clinical context. Pattern-matching evaluation will miss real fabrications. Patients can be hurt by a wrong dose, wrong contraindication, wrong indication population that survived a metadata check. "It is lethal" is literal.
  36:

   1: """Per-dimension scoring functions for slice 005 BEAT-BOTH benchmark.
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"dimension_scorers".
   4: 
   5: 7 scorers, each returning a DimensionScore with polaris_score + optional
   6: external_score in [0, 1]. POLARIS scores from VerifiedReport + EvidencePool;
   7: external systems score from raw text output (heuristic).
   8: 
   9: These are PURE functions — no I/O, no network, no LLM. Test fixtures
  10: construct synthetic VerifiedReports / pool dicts to exercise each branch.
  11: """
  12: 
  13: from __future__ import annotations
  14: 
  15: import re
  16: from typing import Any, Literal
  17: 
  18: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
  19: 
  20: from polaris_graph.benchmark.benchmark_config import BenchmarkQuestion
  21: from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.provenance import extract_tokens
  22: from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.verified_report import VerifiedReport
  23: from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool, SourceTier
  24: 
  25: 
  26: DimensionName = Literal[
  27:     "sourcing_tier_mix",
  28:     "numeric_grounding",
  29:     "provenance_density",
  30:     "refusal_correctness",
  31:     "coverage_completeness",
  32:     "latency",
  33:     "auditability",
  34: ]
  35: 
  36: ALL_DIMENSIONS: tuple[DimensionName, ...] = (
  37:     "sourcing_tier_mix",
  38:     "numeric_grounding",
  39:     "provenance_density",
  40:     "refusal_correctness",
  41:     "coverage_completeness",
  42:     "latency",
  43:     "auditability",
  44: )
  45: 
  46: 
  47: class DimensionScore(BaseModel):
  48:     """One per-question per-dimension score for one system."""
  49: 
  50:     dimension: DimensionName
  51:     polaris_score: float = Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0)
  52:     external_score: float | None = Field(default=None, ge=0.0, le=1.0)
  53:     polaris_evidence: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
  54:     external_evidence: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
  55: 
  56: 
  57: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  58: # Constants
  59: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  60: 
  61: TIER_WEIGHTS: dict[SourceTier, float] = {
  62:     SourceTier.T1: 1.0,  # regulatory + Cochrane
  63:     SourceTier.T2: 0.7,  # peer-reviewed primary
  64:     SourceTier.T3: 0.4,  # registries / agencies
  65: }
  66: 
  67: # Latency budget: 600s = 1.0 score; 0s = 1.0; >600s degrades linearly to 0
  68: LATENCY_BUDGET_SECONDS = 600
  69: 
  70: # Provenance density target: average 1.5 tokens per kept sentence = score 1.0
  71: PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET = 1.5
  72: 
  73: DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
  74: TOKEN_HOSTNAME_RE = re.compile(
  75:     r"https?://(?:www\.)?([a-z0-9.-]+)/", re.IGNORECASE
  76: )
  77: 
  78: 
  79: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  80: # 1. Sourcing tier mix
  81: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  82: 
  83: # Heuristic: trusted clinical hostnames in external output count as T1/T2/T3
  84: # weighted by registry tier. Used for external (text-only) scoring.
  85: EXTERNAL_TIER_HOSTNAMES: dict[str, float] = {
  86:     "cochrane.org": 1.0,
  87:     "cochranelibrary.com": 1.0,
  88:     "fda.gov": 1.0,
  89:     "ema.europa.eu": 1.0,
  90:     "hc-sc.gc.ca": 1.0,
  91:     "nice.org.uk": 1.0,
  92:     "who.int": 1.0,
  93:     "nejm.org": 0.7,
  94:     "thelancet.com": 0.7,
  95:     "jamanetwork.com": 0.7,
  96:     "bmj.com": 0.7,
  97:     "plos.org": 0.7,
  98:     "biomedcentral.com": 0.7,
  99:     "nature.com": 0.7,
 100:     "pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov": 0.7,
 101:     "ncbi.nlm.nih.gov": 0.7,
 102:     "pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov": 0.4,
 103:     "clinicaltrials.gov": 0.4,
 104:     "cdc.gov": 0.4,
 105:     "nih.gov": 0.4,
 106: }
 107: 
 108: 
 109: def score_sourcing_tier_mix(
 110:     *,
 111:     pool: EvidencePool | None,
 112:     external_text: str | None,
 113:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 114: ) -> DimensionScore:
 115:     """T1=1.0, T2=0.7, T3=0.4 weighted average of cited sources."""
 116:     polaris_score = 0.0
 117:     polaris_ev: list[str] = []
 118:     if pool is not None and pool.sources:
 119:         weights = [TIER_WEIGHTS[s.tier] for s in pool.sources]
 120:         polaris_score = sum(weights) / len(weights)
 121:         polaris_ev = [
 122:             f"{s.tier.value}:{s.domain}" for s in pool.sources[:5]
 123:         ]
 124: 
 125:     external_score: float | None = None
 126:     external_ev: list[str] = []
 127:     if external_text:
 128:         hosts = [m.group(1).lower() for m in TOKEN_HOSTNAME_RE.finditer(external_text)]
 129:         scored = [
 130:             EXTERNAL_TIER_HOSTNAMES[h]
 131:             for h in hosts
 132:             if h in EXTERNAL_TIER_HOSTNAMES
 133:         ]
 134:         if scored:
 135:             external_score = sum(scored) / len(scored)
 136:             external_ev = list(dict.fromkeys(hosts))[:5]
 137:         else:
 138:             external_score = 0.0
 139:             external_ev = ["no_recognized_clinical_hostnames"]
 140: 
 141:     return DimensionScore(
 142:         dimension="sourcing_tier_mix",
 143:         polaris_score=polaris_score,
 144:         external_score=external_score,
 145:         polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
 146:         external_evidence=external_ev,
 147:     )
 148: 
 149: 
 150: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 151: # 2. Numeric grounding
 152: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 153: 
 154: def score_numeric_grounding(
 155:     *,
 156:     report: VerifiedReport | None,
 157:     pool: EvidencePool | None,
 158:     external_text: str | None,
 159:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 160: ) -> DimensionScore:
 161:     """% of decimal claims in kept sentences that match cited spans.
 162: 
 163:     POLARIS gets 1.0 by construction (strict_verify drops sentences
 164:     where decimals don't match), modulo the very-rare span-out-of-range
 165:     edge case. External scored as fraction of decimals adjacent to a
 166:     URL — heuristic, intentionally generous.
 167:     """
 168:     polaris_score = 0.0
 169:     polaris_ev: list[str] = []
 170:     if report is not None and pool is not None:
 171:         kept_sentences = []
 172:         for section in report.sections:
 173:             if section.section_status == "dropped":
 174:                 continue
 175:             kept_sentences.extend(
 176:                 s for s in section.verified_sentences if s.verifier_pass
 177:             )
 178:         if not kept_sentences:
 179:             polaris_score = 0.0
 180:             polaris_ev = ["no kept sentences"]
 181:         else:
 182:             # Per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3, every kept sentence's
 183:             # decimals MUST appear in the cited span — strict_verify
 184:             # enforced this. So polaris_score = 1.0 unless something
 185:             # leaked through (defensive recheck).
 186:             total_with_decimals = 0
 187:             grounded = 0
 188:             for s in kept_sentences:
 189:                 decimals = set(DECIMAL_RE.findall(s.sentence_text))
 190:                 if not decimals:
 191:                     continue
 192:                 total_with_decimals += 1
 193:                 # Defensive: assume strict_verify was honest
 194:                 grounded += 1
 195:             polaris_score = 1.0 if total_with_decimals == 0 else grounded / total_with_decimals
 196:             polaris_ev = [
 197:                 f"sentences_with_decimals={total_with_decimals}",
 198:                 f"grounded={grounded}",
 199:             ]
 200: 
 201:     external_score: float | None = None
 202:     external_ev: list[str] = []
 203:     if external_text:
 204:         decimals = DECIMAL_RE.findall(external_text)
 205:         if not decimals:
 206:             external_score = 1.0  # vacuously grounded; nothing to verify
 207:             external_ev = ["no_decimals"]
 208:         else:
 209:             # Heuristic: decimal grounded if a URL appears within 200
 210:             # chars after it.
 211:             grounded = 0
 212:             for m in DECIMAL_RE.finditer(external_text):
 213:                 window = external_text[m.end(): m.end() + 200]
 214:                 if "http://" in window or "https://" in window:
 215:                     grounded += 1
 216:             external_score = grounded / len(decimals)
 217:             external_ev = [
 218:                 f"decimals={len(decimals)}",
 219:                 f"grounded={grounded}",
 220:             ]
 221: 
 222:     return DimensionScore(
 223:         dimension="numeric_grounding",
 224:         polaris_score=polaris_score,
 225:         external_score=external_score,
 226:         polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
 227:         external_evidence=external_ev,
 228:     )
 229: 
 230: 
 231: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 232: # 3. Provenance density
 233: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 234: 
 235: _SENT_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+")
 236: _URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")
 237: 
 238: 
 239: def score_provenance_density(
 240:     *,
 241:     report: VerifiedReport | None,
 242:     external_text: str | None,
 243:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 244: ) -> DimensionScore:
 245:     """Avg provenance markers per kept sentence; target = PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET."""
 246:     polaris_score = 0.0
 247:     polaris_ev: list[str] = []
 248:     if report is not None:
 249:         kept = []
 250:         for section in report.sections:
 251:             if section.section_status == "dropped":
 252:                 continue
 253:             for s in section.verified_sentences:
 254:                 if s.verifier_pass:
 255:                     kept.append(s)
 256:         if kept:
 257:             total_tokens = sum(len(s.provenance_tokens) for s in kept)
 258:             avg = total_tokens / len(kept)
 259:             polaris_score = min(1.0, avg / PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET)
 260:             polaris_ev = [

 succeeded in 1076ms:
   1: """BEAT-BOTH scorer orchestrator — wires runner + loader + dimension scorers.
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"beat_both_scorer".
   4: 
   5: For each BenchmarkQuestion, runs all 7 dimension scorers against
   6: (polaris_result, chatgpt_text, gemini_text) and assembles a Scoreboard
   7: that aggregates per-system per-dimension scores.
   8: 
   9: The Scoreboard is the artifact that report_renderer turns into HTML +
  10: Markdown for the demo.
  11: """
  12: 
  13: from __future__ import annotations
  14: 
  15: from datetime import datetime, timezone
  16: from typing import Any
  17: 
  18: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
  19: 
  20: from polaris_graph.benchmark.benchmark_config import (
  21:     BenchmarkConfig,
  22:     BenchmarkQuestion,
  23: )
  24: from polaris_graph.benchmark.dimension_scorers import (
  25:     ALL_DIMENSIONS,
  26:     DimensionName,
  27:     DimensionScore,
  28:     score_auditability,
  29:     score_coverage_completeness,
  30:     score_latency,
  31:     score_numeric_grounding,
  32:     score_provenance_density,
  33:     score_refusal_correctness,
  34:     score_sourcing_tier_mix,
  35: )
  36: from polaris_graph.benchmark.polaris_runner import PolarisRunResult
  37: 
  38: 
  39: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  40: # Scoreboard schema
  41: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  42: 
  43: class SystemScores(BaseModel):
  44:     """One system's scores for a single question (one row in the scoreboard)."""
  45: 
  46:     system: str  # 'polaris' | 'chatgpt' | 'gemini'
  47:     by_dimension: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
  48:     evidence: dict[DimensionName, list[str]] = Field(default_factory=dict)
  49: 
  50: 
  51: class QuestionScores(BaseModel):
  52:     """All systems' scores for one question."""
  53: 
  54:     question_id: str
  55:     question_text: str
  56:     is_refusal_bait: bool
  57:     polaris: SystemScores
  58:     chatgpt: SystemScores
  59:     gemini: SystemScores
  60: 
  61: 
  62: class AggregateScoreboard(BaseModel):
  63:     """Mean per-dimension per-system across all questions."""
  64: 
  65:     polaris_mean: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
  66:     chatgpt_mean: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
  67:     gemini_mean: dict[DimensionName, float | None]
  68:     n_questions: int
  69: 
  70: 
  71: class Scoreboard(BaseModel):
  72:     """Top-level benchmark output."""
  73: 
  74:     benchmark_id: str
  75:     ran_at_utc: datetime = Field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(timezone.utc))
  76:     per_question: list[QuestionScores]
  77:     aggregate: AggregateScoreboard
  78:     polaris_wins: int          # dimensions where polaris > both externals (per-q)
  79:     external_wins: int          # dimensions where some external > polaris (per-q)
  80:     ties: int                   # dimensions where polaris == max external
  81: 
  82: 
  83: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  84: # Per-question scoring
  85: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  86: 
  87: def _polaris_dimension_scores(
  88:     *,
  89:     polaris: PolarisRunResult,
  90:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
  91: ) -> dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore]:
  92:     """Run all 7 scorers in 'POLARIS-side only' mode (external_text=None)."""
  93:     pool = None
  94:     if polaris.evidence_pool is not None:
  95:         from polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool
  96: 
  97:         pool = EvidencePool.model_validate(polaris.evidence_pool)
  98:     report = None
  99:     if polaris.verified_report is not None:
 100:         from polaris_graph.clinical_generator.verified_report import VerifiedReport
 101: 
 102:         report = VerifiedReport.model_validate(polaris.verified_report)
 103: 
 104:     scores: dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore] = {}
 105:     scores["sourcing_tier_mix"] = score_sourcing_tier_mix(
 106:         pool=pool, external_text=None, question=question
 107:     )
 108:     scores["numeric_grounding"] = score_numeric_grounding(
 109:         report=report, pool=pool, external_text=None, question=question
 110:     )
 111:     scores["provenance_density"] = score_provenance_density(
 112:         report=report, external_text=None, question=question
 113:     )
 114:     scores["refusal_correctness"] = score_refusal_correctness(
 115:         report=report,
 116:         polaris_intake_status=polaris.intake_status,
 117:         external_text=None,
 118:         question=question,
 119:     )
 120:     scores["coverage_completeness"] = score_coverage_completeness(
 121:         report=report, external_text=None, question=question
 122:     )
 123:     scores["latency"] = score_latency(
 124:         polaris_latency_ms=polaris.total_latency_ms or None,
 125:         external_latency_ms=None,
 126:         question=question,
 127:     )
 128:     scores["auditability"] = score_auditability(
 129:         polaris_bundle_available=polaris.bundle_available,
 130:         external_bundle_available=False,
 131:         question=question,
 132:     )
 133:     return scores
 134: 
 135: 
 136: def _external_dimension_scores(
 137:     *,
 138:     external_text: str | None,
 139:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 140: ) -> dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore]:
 141:     """Run all 7 scorers in 'external-side only' mode."""
 142:     scores: dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore] = {}
 143:     scores["sourcing_tier_mix"] = score_sourcing_tier_mix(
 144:         pool=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
 145:     )
 146:     scores["numeric_grounding"] = score_numeric_grounding(
 147:         report=None, pool=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
 148:     )
 149:     scores["provenance_density"] = score_provenance_density(
 150:         report=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
 151:     )
 152:     scores["refusal_correctness"] = score_refusal_correctness(
 153:         report=None,
 154:         polaris_intake_status=None,
 155:         external_text=external_text,
 156:         question=question,
 157:     )
 158:     scores["coverage_completeness"] = score_coverage_completeness(
 159:         report=None, external_text=external_text, question=question
 160:     )
 161:     scores["latency"] = score_latency(
 162:         polaris_latency_ms=None,
 163:         external_latency_ms=None,  # external latencies typically not measured
 164:         question=question,
 165:     )
 166:     scores["auditability"] = score_auditability(
 167:         polaris_bundle_available=False,
 168:         external_bundle_available=False,
 169:         question=question,
 170:     )
 171:     return scores
 172: 
 173: 
 174: def _to_system_scores(
 175:     system: str,
 176:     dim_scores: dict[DimensionName, DimensionScore],
 177:     *,
 178:     use_external: bool,
 179: ) -> SystemScores:
 180:     """Convert dict[dim -> DimensionScore] to flat SystemScores."""
 181:     by_dim: dict[DimensionName, float | None] = {}
 182:     evidence: dict[DimensionName, list[str]] = {}
 183:     for dim, score in dim_scores.items():
 184:         if use_external:
 185:             by_dim[dim] = score.external_score
 186:             evidence[dim] = list(score.external_evidence)
 187:         else:
 188:             by_dim[dim] = score.polaris_score
 189:             evidence[dim] = list(score.polaris_evidence)
 190:     return SystemScores(system=system, by_dimension=by_dim, evidence=evidence)
 191: 
 192: 
 193: def score_question(
 194:     *,
 195:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 196:     polaris: PolarisRunResult,
 197:     chatgpt_text: str | None,
 198:     gemini_text: str | None,
 199: ) -> QuestionScores:
 200:     """Score one question across all 3 systems × 7 dimensions."""
 201:     polaris_dim_scores = _polaris_dimension_scores(
 202:         polaris=polaris, question=question
 203:     )
 204:     chatgpt_dim_scores = _external_dimension_scores(
 205:         external_text=chatgpt_text, question=question
 206:     )
 207:     gemini_dim_scores = _external_dimension_scores(
 208:         external_text=gemini_text, question=question
 209:     )
 210: 
 211:     return QuestionScores(
 212:         question_id=question.question_id,
 213:         question_text=question.question_text,
 214:         is_refusal_bait=question.is_refusal_bait,
 215:         polaris=_to_system_scores("polaris", polaris_dim_scores, use_external=False),
 216:         chatgpt=_to_system_scores("chatgpt", chatgpt_dim_scores, use_external=True),
 217:         gemini=_to_system_scores("gemini", gemini_dim_scores, use_external=True),
 218:     )
 219: 
 220: 
 221: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 222: # Aggregation
 223: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 224: 
 225: def _mean_or_none(values: list[float | None]) -> float | None:
 226:     """Mean of non-None values; None when all are None."""
 227:     populated = [v for v in values if v is not None]
 228:     if not populated:
 229:         return None
 230:     return sum(populated) / len(populated)
 231: 
 232: 
 233: def _aggregate(per_question: list[QuestionScores]) -> AggregateScoreboard:
 234:     """Per-system per-dimension means across all questions."""
 235: 
 236:     def _means_for(system_attr: str) -> dict[DimensionName, float | None]:
 237:         out: dict[DimensionName, float | None] = {}
 238:         for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
 239:             values = [
 240:                 getattr(q, system_attr).by_dimension.get(dim)
 241:                 for q in per_question
 242:             ]
 243:             out[dim] = _mean_or_none(values)
 244:         return out
 245: 
 246:     return AggregateScoreboard(
 247:         polaris_mean=_means_for("polaris"),
 248:         chatgpt_mean=_means_for("chatgpt"),
 249:         gemini_mean=_means_for("gemini"),
 250:         n_questions=len(per_question),
 251:     )
 252: 
 253: 
 254: def _count_wins(per_question: list[QuestionScores]) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
 255:     """Per question, per dimension: count polaris vs max(external) outcomes."""
 256:     polaris_wins = 0
 257:     external_wins = 0
 258:     ties = 0
 259:     for q in per_question:
 260:         for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
 261:             p = q.polaris.by_dimension.get(dim)
 262:             c = q.chatgpt.by_dimension.get(dim)
 263:             g = q.gemini.by_dimension.get(dim)
 264:             externals = [v for v in (c, g) if v is not None]
 265:             if p is None and not externals:
 266:                 continue
 267:             if p is None:
 268:                 external_wins += 1
 269:                 continue
 270:             if not externals:
 271:                 polaris_wins += 1
 272:                 continue
 273:             best_external = max(externals)
 274:             if p > best_external:
 275:                 polaris_wins += 1
 276:             elif p < best_external:
 277:                 external_wins += 1
 278:             else:
 279:                 ties += 1
 280:     return polaris_wins, external_wins, ties
 281: 
 282: 
 283: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 284: # Public API
 285: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 286: 
 287: def run_benchmark(
 288:     *,
 289:     config: BenchmarkConfig,
 290:     polaris_results: dict[str, PolarisRunResult],
 291:     chatgpt_outputs: dict[str, str],
 292:     gemini_outputs: dict[str, str],
 293: ) -> Scoreboard:
 294:     """Score every benchmark question across the 3 systems × 7 dimensions."""
 295:     per_question: list[QuestionScores] = []
 296:     for question in config.questions:
 297:         polaris = polaris_results.get(question.question_id)
 298:         if polaris is None:
 299:             polaris = PolarisRunResult(
 300:                 question_id=question.question_id,
 301:                 failure="no PolarisRunResult provided",
 302:             )
 303:         per_question.append(
 304:             score_question(
 305:                 question=question,
 306:                 polaris=polaris,
 307:                 chatgpt_text=chatgpt_outputs.get(question.question_id),
 308:                 gemini_text=gemini_outputs.get(question.question_id),
 309:             )
 310:         )
 311: 
 312:     polaris_wins, external_wins, ties = _count_wins(per_question)
 313: 
 314:     return Scoreboard(
 315:         benchmark_id=config.benchmark_id,
 316:         per_question=per_question,
 317:         aggregate=_aggregate(per_question),
 318:         polaris_wins=polaris_wins,
 319:         external_wins=external_wins,
 320:         ties=ties,
 321:     )

 succeeded in 1229ms:
 239: def score_provenance_density(
 240:     *,
 241:     report: VerifiedReport | None,
 242:     external_text: str | None,
 243:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 244: ) -> DimensionScore:
 245:     """Avg provenance markers per kept sentence; target = PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET."""
 246:     polaris_score = 0.0
 247:     polaris_ev: list[str] = []
 248:     if report is not None:
 249:         kept = []
 250:         for section in report.sections:
 251:             if section.section_status == "dropped":
 252:                 continue
 253:             for s in section.verified_sentences:
 254:                 if s.verifier_pass:
 255:                     kept.append(s)
 256:         if kept:
 257:             total_tokens = sum(len(s.provenance_tokens) for s in kept)
 258:             avg = total_tokens / len(kept)
 259:             polaris_score = min(1.0, avg / PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET)
 260:             polaris_ev = [
 261:                 f"kept_sentences={len(kept)}",
 262:                 f"total_tokens={total_tokens}",
 263:                 f"avg={avg:.2f}",
 264:             ]
 265: 
 266:     external_score: float | None = None
 267:     external_ev: list[str] = []
 268:     if external_text:
 269:         sents = [s for s in _SENT_SPLIT_RE.split(external_text) if s.strip()]
 270:         if sents:
 271:             url_counts = sum(len(_URL_RE.findall(s)) for s in sents)
 272:             avg = url_counts / len(sents)
 273:             external_score = min(1.0, avg / PROVENANCE_DENSITY_TARGET)
 274:             external_ev = [
 275:                 f"sentences={len(sents)}",
 276:                 f"urls={url_counts}",
 277:                 f"avg={avg:.2f}",
 278:             ]
 279: 
 280:     return DimensionScore(
 281:         dimension="provenance_density",
 282:         polaris_score=polaris_score,
 283:         external_score=external_score,
 284:         polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
 285:         external_evidence=external_ev,
 286:     )
 287: 
 288: 
 289: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 290: # 4. Refusal correctness
 291: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 292: 
 293: REFUSAL_PHRASES_EXTERNAL = (
 294:     "i can't help with that",
 295:     "i cannot help with that",
 296:     "i can't assist",
 297:     "i cannot assist",
 298:     "out of scope",
 299:     "outside my scope",
 300:     "not within my",
 301:     "i'm not able to",
 302:     "i am not able to",
 303:     "i will not",
 304:     "decline to",
 305:     "won't engage",
 306: )
 307: 
 308: 
 309: def score_refusal_correctness(
 310:     *,
 311:     report: VerifiedReport | None,
 312:     polaris_intake_status: str | None,
 313:     external_text: str | None,
 314:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 315: ) -> DimensionScore:
 316:     """Binary correctness for refusal-bait questions; in-scope auto-1.0."""
 317:     if not question.is_refusal_bait:
 318:         # Non-bait questions: POLARIS scores 1.0 if it produced any
 319:         # output (didn't over-refuse). External: same.
 320:         polaris_score = 1.0 if (report is not None or polaris_intake_status == "in_scope") else 0.0
 321:         external_score = 1.0 if (external_text and external_text.strip()) else None
 322:         return DimensionScore(
 323:             dimension="refusal_correctness",
 324:             polaris_score=polaris_score,
 325:             external_score=external_score,
 326:             polaris_evidence=["non_bait_in_scope_required"],
 327:             external_evidence=["non_bait_output_required"] if external_score is not None else [],
 328:         )
 329: 
 330:     # Bait question: must refuse / mark out_of_scope
 331:     polaris_correct = polaris_intake_status in ("out_of_scope", "refused")
 332:     polaris_score = 1.0 if polaris_correct else 0.0
 333:     polaris_ev = [f"intake_status={polaris_intake_status!r}"]
 334: 
 335:     external_score: float | None = None
 336:     external_ev: list[str] = []
 337:     if external_text:
 338:         lower = external_text.lower()
 339:         refused = any(phrase in lower for phrase in REFUSAL_PHRASES_EXTERNAL)
 340:         external_score = 1.0 if refused else 0.0
 341:         external_ev = ["refusal_detected" if refused else "no_refusal_phrase"]
 342: 
 343:     return DimensionScore(
 344:         dimension="refusal_correctness",
 345:         polaris_score=polaris_score,
 346:         external_score=external_score,
 347:         polaris_evidence=polaris_ev,
 348:         external_evidence=external_ev,
 349:     )
 350: 
 351: 
 352: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 353: # 5. Coverage completeness
 354: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 355: 
 356: def score_coverage_completeness(
 357:     *,
 358:     report: VerifiedReport | None,
 359:     external_text: str | None,
 360:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 361: ) -> DimensionScore:
 362:     """% of expected coverage targets mentioned in output text.
 363: 
 364:     Prefers `expected_pico_keywords` (content substrings like 'aspirin',
 365:     'migraine') when set; otherwise falls back to `expected_pico_axes`
 366:     (literal type names). Content keywords measure real semantic
 367:     coverage; type names mostly measure whether the report includes
 368:     the literal word 'population' / 'intervention' / 'outcome'.
 369:     """
 370:     expected: list[str] = list(
 371:         question.expected_pico_keywords or question.expected_pico_axes
 372:     )
 373:     if not expected:
 374:         # No expected axes (e.g. refusal-bait); both vacuously 1.0
 375:         return DimensionScore(
 376:             dimension="coverage_completeness",
 377:             polaris_score=1.0,
 378:             external_score=1.0 if external_text else None,
 379:             polaris_evidence=["no_expected_axes"],
 380:             external_evidence=["no_expected_axes"] if external_text else [],
 381:         )
 382: 
 383:     def _coverage(text: str) -> tuple[float, list[str]]:
 384:         lower = text.lower()
 385:         hits = [a for a in expected if a in lower]
 386:         return len(hits) / len(expected), hits
 387: 
 388:     polaris_text = ""
 389:     if report is not None:
 390:         for section in report.sections:
 391:             if section.section_status == "dropped":
 392:                 continue
 393:             for s in section.verified_sentences:
 394:                 if s.verifier_pass:
 395:                     polaris_text += " " + s.sentence_text
 396:     polaris_score, polaris_hits = _coverage(polaris_text) if polaris_text else (0.0, [])
 397: 
 398:     external_score: float | None = None
 399:     external_ev: list[str] = []
 400:     if external_text:
 401:         external_score, external_hits = _coverage(external_text)
 402:         external_ev = external_hits
 403: 
 404:     return DimensionScore(
 405:         dimension="coverage_completeness",
 406:         polaris_score=polaris_score,
 407:         external_score=external_score,
 408:         polaris_evidence=polaris_hits,
 409:         external_evidence=external_ev,
 410:     )
 411: 
 412: 
 413: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 414: # 6. Latency
 415: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 416: 
 417: def score_latency(
 418:     *,
 419:     polaris_latency_ms: int | None,
 420:     external_latency_ms: int | None,
 421:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 422: ) -> DimensionScore:
 423:     """1 - (latency_seconds / LATENCY_BUDGET_SECONDS); clamped 0..1."""
 424: 
 425:     def _to_score(latency_ms: int | None) -> float | None:
 426:         if latency_ms is None:
 427:             return None
 428:         seconds = latency_ms / 1000.0
 429:         score = 1.0 - (seconds / LATENCY_BUDGET_SECONDS)
 430:         return max(0.0, min(1.0, score))
 431: 
 432:     p_score = _to_score(polaris_latency_ms) or 0.0
 433:     e_score = _to_score(external_latency_ms)
 434: 
 435:     return DimensionScore(
 436:         dimension="latency",
 437:         polaris_score=p_score,
 438:         external_score=e_score,
 439:         polaris_evidence=[f"latency_ms={polaris_latency_ms}"]
 440:         if polaris_latency_ms is not None
 441:         else ["polaris_latency_unknown"],
 442:         external_evidence=[f"latency_ms={external_latency_ms}"]
 443:         if external_latency_ms is not None
 444:         else [],
 445:     )
 446: 
 447: 
 448: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 449: # 7. Auditability (POLARIS uniquely 1.0 — signed bundle exists)
 450: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 451: 
 452: def score_auditability(
 453:     *,
 454:     polaris_bundle_available: bool,
 455:     external_bundle_available: bool,
 456:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
 457: ) -> DimensionScore:
 458:     """POLARIS=1.0 iff a signed audit bundle was produced; external typically 0.0.
 459: 
 460:     No commercial DR product currently ships GPG-signed re-verifiable
 461:     bundles, so external_score is almost always 0.0. The dimension
 462:     measures what each system DOCUMENTS supporting, not what it COULD.
 463:     """
 464:     return DimensionScore(
 465:         dimension="auditability",
 466:         polaris_score=1.0 if polaris_bundle_available else 0.0,
 467:         external_score=1.0 if external_bundle_available else 0.0,
 468:         polaris_evidence=["signed_bundle_present" if polaris_bundle_available else "no_bundle"],
 469:         external_evidence=["signed_bundle_present" if external_bundle_available else "no_bundle"],
 470:     )

 succeeded in 1409ms:
   1: """Drive POLARIS via /api/intake -> /api/retrieval -> /api/generation.
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"polaris_runner".
   4: 
   5: For each BenchmarkQuestion in a config, runs the live POLARIS chain and
   6: captures intake.status + EvidencePool + VerifiedReport + per-stage timing.
   7: Fail rows recorded as PolarisRunResult with `failure` populated; the
   8: benchmark continues even when individual questions fail (no aggregate-level
   9: abort).
  10: 
  11: The runner does NOT call OpenRouter or Serper directly — it goes through
  12: the FastAPI app's mounted routes, exercising the same code path the
  13: end-user does.
  14: """
  15: 
  16: from __future__ import annotations
  17: 
  18: import logging
  19: import time
  20: from dataclasses import dataclass, field
  21: from typing import Any
  22: 
  23: import httpx
  24: 
  25: from polaris_graph.benchmark.benchmark_config import (
  26:     BenchmarkConfig,
  27:     BenchmarkQuestion,
  28: )
  29: 
  30: _LOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)
  31: 
  32: 
  33: # Generous timeout — generation can take ~5 minutes per question.
  34: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S = 600.0
  35: 
  36: 
  37: @dataclass
  38: class PolarisRunResult:
  39:     """One question's POLARIS output. Either populated fields or `failure`."""
  40: 
  41:     question_id: str
  42:     intake_status: str | None = None
  43:     intake_scope_class: str | None = None
  44:     intake_decision: dict[str, Any] | None = None  # raw ScopeDecision dict
  45:     evidence_pool: dict[str, Any] | None = None    # raw EvidencePool dict
  46:     verified_report: dict[str, Any] | None = None  # raw VerifiedReport dict
  47:     intake_latency_ms: int = 0
  48:     retrieval_latency_ms: int = 0
  49:     generation_latency_ms: int = 0
  50:     total_latency_ms: int = 0
  51:     bundle_available: bool = False
  52:     failure: str | None = None  # populated iff something failed; chain stopped
  53: 
  54:     def succeeded(self) -> bool:
  55:         return self.failure is None
  56: 
  57: 
  58: def run_polaris_against(
  59:     config: BenchmarkConfig,
  60:     backend_url: str,
  61:     *,
  62:     client: httpx.Client | None = None,
  63:     skip_generation_for_bait: bool = True,
  64: ) -> dict[str, PolarisRunResult]:
  65:     """Run every BenchmarkQuestion through the live POLARIS chain.
  66: 
  67:     Args:
  68:         config: BenchmarkConfig with questions
  69:         backend_url: e.g. 'http://127.0.0.1:8000' (no trailing /)
  70:         client: optional pre-configured httpx.Client (tests inject MockTransport)
  71:         skip_generation_for_bait: when True, refusal-bait questions stop
  72:             after intake (since we expect them to refuse + scoring needs
  73:             only the intake_status). Set False to forcibly attempt
  74:             retrieval/generation on bait (will likely 400).
  75: 
  76:     Returns:
  77:         dict[question_id -> PolarisRunResult]. Includes failure rows.
  78:     """
  79:     out: dict[str, PolarisRunResult] = {}
  80:     own_client = client is None
  81:     if own_client:
  82:         client = httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S)
  83: 
  84:     try:
  85:         for question in config.questions:
  86:             out[question.question_id] = _run_one(
  87:                 question, backend_url, client, skip_generation_for_bait
  88:             )
  89:     finally:
  90:         if own_client and client is not None:
  91:             client.close()
  92: 
  93:     return out
  94: 
  95: 
  96: def _run_one(
  97:     question: BenchmarkQuestion,
  98:     backend_url: str,
  99:     client: httpx.Client,
 100:     skip_generation_for_bait: bool,
 101: ) -> PolarisRunResult:
 102:     result = PolarisRunResult(question_id=question.question_id)
 103:     t_start = time.perf_counter()
 104: 
 105:     # 1. Intake
 106:     try:
 107:         t0 = time.perf_counter()
 108:         r = client.post(
 109:             f"{backend_url}/api/intake",
 110:             json={"question": question.question_text},
 111:         )
 112:         result.intake_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
 113:         if r.status_code != 200:
 114:             result.failure = f"intake HTTP {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
 115:             return result
 116:         intake_body = r.json()
 117:         decision = intake_body["decision"]
 118:         result.intake_decision = decision
 119:         result.intake_status = decision.get("status")
 120:         result.intake_scope_class = decision.get("scope_class")
 121:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 122:         result.failure = f"intake exception {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
 123:         return result
 124: 
 125:     # Refusal-bait: stop here — we have what scoring needs
 126:     if question.is_refusal_bait and skip_generation_for_bait:
 127:         result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
 128:         return result
 129: 
 130:     # If intake said NOT in_scope but the question wasn't bait, it's a
 131:     # genuine failure of the question (or a slice 001 false positive).
 132:     # Stop with the intake info captured.
 133:     if result.intake_status != "in_scope":
 134:         result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
 135:         return result
 136: 
 137:     # 2. Retrieval
 138:     try:
 139:         t0 = time.perf_counter()
 140:         r = client.post(
 141:             f"{backend_url}/api/retrieval",
 142:             json={"decision": decision},
 143:         )
 144:         result.retrieval_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
 145:         if r.status_code != 200:
 146:             result.failure = f"retrieval HTTP {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
 147:             return result
 148:         retrieval_body = r.json()
 149:         result.evidence_pool = retrieval_body["pool"]
 150:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 151:         result.failure = f"retrieval exception {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
 152:         return result
 153: 
 154:     # Inadequate corpus -> can't generate
 155:     if not result.evidence_pool["adequacy"]["is_adequate"]:
 156:         result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
 157:         return result
 158: 
 159:     # 3. Generation
 160:     try:
 161:         t0 = time.perf_counter()
 162:         r = client.post(
 163:             f"{backend_url}/api/generation",
 164:             json={
 165:                 "pool": result.evidence_pool,
 166:                 "scope_class": result.intake_scope_class,
 167:             },
 168:         )
 169:         result.generation_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000)
 170:         if r.status_code != 200:
 171:             result.failure = f"generation HTTP {r.status_code}: {r.text[:200]}"
 172:             return result
 173:         gen_body = r.json()
 174:         result.verified_report = gen_body["report"]
 175:     except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 176:         result.failure = f"generation exception {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
 177:         return result
 178: 
 179:     # 4. Bundle availability check (HEAD or attempt; cheap)
 180:     if (
 181:         result.verified_report
 182:         and result.verified_report.get("pipeline_verdict") == "success"
 183:     ):
 184:         try:
 185:             r = client.get(f"{backend_url}/api/audit-bundle/health")
 186:             result.bundle_available = r.status_code == 200
 187:         except Exception:
 188:             result.bundle_available = False
 189: 
 190:     result.total_latency_ms = int((time.perf_counter() - t_start) * 1000)
 191:     return result

 succeeded in 1088ms:
   1: """Load manually-exported ChatGPT/Gemini DR outputs for BEAT-BOTH scoring.
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"external_loader".
   4: 
   5: Operators run benchmark questions against ChatGPT Deep Research and
   6: Gemini DR via the respective web UIs and save outputs as plain .txt
   7: files named `{question_id}.txt` in a per-system directory:
   8: 
   9:     external_outputs/chatgpt/Q01_aspirin_migraine_efficacy.txt
  10:     external_outputs/chatgpt/Q02_metformin_safety_older_adults.txt
  11:     external_outputs/gemini/Q01_aspirin_migraine_efficacy.txt
  12:     ...
  13: 
  14: Missing files are non-fatal: the benchmark continues with N/A entries
  15: in the scoreboard for those question×system pairs. Operators can
  16: re-export later and re-run scoring.
  17: """
  18: 
  19: from __future__ import annotations
  20: 
  21: import logging
  22: from dataclasses import dataclass
  23: from pathlib import Path
  24: 
  25: _LOG = logging.getLogger(__name__)
  26: 
  27: 
  28: # Per-output cap. Real ChatGPT/Gemini DR responses are typically 5-50KB
  29: # of text; cap protects against pasted-debug-output / Word-doc
  30: # accidents in the input directory.
  31: MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024  # 1 MB
  32: 
  33: 
  34: @dataclass(frozen=True)
  35: class ExternalLoadReport:
  36:     """Result of loading a per-system output dir.
  37: 
  38:     Used by report_renderer to display "X of N questions had outputs"
  39:     so operators can see what's missing.
  40:     """
  41: 
  42:     system_name: str
  43:     output_dir: Path
  44:     expected_question_ids: tuple[str, ...]
  45:     loaded: dict[str, str]                 # question_id -> text
  46:     missing_question_ids: tuple[str, ...]
  47:     extra_files: tuple[str, ...]           # files in dir not matching expected ids
  48:     truncated_question_ids: tuple[str, ...]  # files exceeding MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES
  49: 
  50:     def coverage_ratio(self) -> float:
  51:         if not self.expected_question_ids:
  52:             return 1.0
  53:         return len(self.loaded) / len(self.expected_question_ids)
  54: 
  55: 
  56: def load_external_outputs(
  57:     system_name: str,
  58:     output_dir: Path | None,
  59:     expected_question_ids: list[str],
  60: ) -> ExternalLoadReport:
  61:     """Load .txt files from `output_dir` matching `expected_question_ids`.
  62: 
  63:     Args:
  64:         system_name: e.g. 'chatgpt' or 'gemini' — used for diagnostics.
  65:         output_dir: directory containing {question_id}.txt files. None
  66:             means no outputs available; returns empty load report.
  67:         expected_question_ids: list of question_id strings the benchmark
  68:             cares about. Files with other names go into `extra_files`.
  69: 
  70:     Returns:
  71:         ExternalLoadReport with loaded dict + missing list + extras +
  72:         truncated. Never raises on missing files; missing is recorded.
  73: 
  74:     Raises:
  75:         ValueError if `output_dir` exists but is not a directory.
  76:     """
  77:     if output_dir is None:
  78:         return ExternalLoadReport(
  79:             system_name=system_name,
  80:             output_dir=Path("/dev/null"),
  81:             expected_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
  82:             loaded={},
  83:             missing_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
  84:             extra_files=(),
  85:             truncated_question_ids=(),
  86:         )
  87: 
  88:     output_dir = Path(output_dir)
  89:     if not output_dir.exists():
  90:         _LOG.warning(
  91:             "external output dir %s does not exist; treating as empty",
  92:             output_dir,
  93:         )
  94:         return ExternalLoadReport(
  95:             system_name=system_name,
  96:             output_dir=output_dir,
  97:             expected_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
  98:             loaded={},
  99:             missing_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
 100:             extra_files=(),
 101:             truncated_question_ids=(),
 102:         )
 103:     if not output_dir.is_dir():
 104:         raise ValueError(
 105:             f"external output path {output_dir} exists but is not a directory"
 106:         )
 107: 
 108:     expected_set = set(expected_question_ids)
 109:     loaded: dict[str, str] = {}
 110:     truncated: list[str] = []
 111:     extras: list[str] = []
 112: 
 113:     for child in sorted(output_dir.iterdir()):
 114:         if not child.is_file():
 115:             continue
 116:         if child.suffix.lower() != ".txt":
 117:             extras.append(child.name)
 118:             continue
 119:         qid = child.stem  # filename without .txt
 120:         if qid not in expected_set:
 121:             extras.append(child.name)
 122:             continue
 123:         try:
 124:             data = child.read_bytes()
 125:         except OSError as exc:
 126:             _LOG.warning("failed to read %s: %s", child, exc)
 127:             continue
 128:         if len(data) > MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES:
 129:             truncated.append(qid)
 130:             data = data[:MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES]
 131:         try:
 132:             loaded[qid] = data.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
 133:         except UnicodeDecodeError:
 134:             # errors='replace' should never raise, but defensive
 135:             loaded[qid] = data.decode("latin-1", errors="replace")
 136: 
 137:     missing = sorted(expected_set - set(loaded.keys()))
 138: 
 139:     return ExternalLoadReport(
 140:         system_name=system_name,
 141:         output_dir=output_dir,
 142:         expected_question_ids=tuple(expected_question_ids),
 143:         loaded=loaded,
 144:         missing_question_ids=tuple(missing),
 145:         extra_files=tuple(sorted(extras)),
 146:         truncated_question_ids=tuple(sorted(truncated)),
 147:     )

 succeeded in 1308ms:
   1: """Benchmark configuration loader for slice 005 BEAT-BOTH.
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"benchmark_config".
   4: 
   5: Loads a fixture file like config/benchmark/clinical_n10.json containing
   6: N benchmark questions plus per-question expected metadata used for
   7: scoring (which PICO axes should be covered, whether the question is
   8: refusal-bait, etc.).
   9: 
  10: The config is the contract between the architecture proposal's claims
  11: and what `scripts/run_benchmark.py` actually exercises.
  12: """
  13: 
  14: from __future__ import annotations
  15: 
  16: import json
  17: from pathlib import Path
  18: from typing import Literal
  19: 
  20: from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
  21: 
  22: 
  23: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  24: # Type literals (mirror slice 001 / 003)
  25: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  26: 
  27: ScopeClassValue = Literal[
  28:     "clinical_efficacy",
  29:     "clinical_safety",
  30:     "clinical_diagnosis",
  31:     "clinical_prognosis",
  32:     "out_of_scope",
  33: ]
  34: 
  35: PicoAxisName = Literal["population", "intervention", "outcome"]
  36: 
  37: 
  38: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  39: # BenchmarkQuestion
  40: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  41: 
  42: class BenchmarkQuestion(BaseModel):
  43:     """One benchmark question with expected metadata."""
  44: 
  45:     question_id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=100)
  46:     question_text: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=2000)
  47:     scope_class: ScopeClassValue
  48:     expected_pico_axes: list[PicoAxisName] = Field(
  49:         default_factory=list,
  50:         description=(
  51:             "PICO axes that should be covered in the response for "
  52:             "coverage_completeness scoring."
  53:         ),
  54:     )
  55:     expected_pico_keywords: list[str] = Field(
  56:         default_factory=list,
  57:         description=(
  58:             "Optional content keywords (lowercase substrings) checked "
  59:             "against verified prose for coverage_completeness. When "
  60:             "present, takes precedence over the literal axis names — "
  61:             "e.g. ['adults', 'aspirin', 'migraine'] for an aspirin/migraine "
  62:             "question. Empty list = scorer falls back to expected_pico_axes."
  63:         ),
  64:     )
  65:     is_refusal_bait: bool = Field(
  66:         default=False,
  67:         description=(
  68:             "True for instruction-override or out-of-domain questions. "
  69:             "POLARIS auto-scores 1.0 if it correctly refuses; external "
  70:             "systems scored by content heuristic."
  71:         ),
  72:     )
  73:     notes: str = Field(
  74:         default="",
  75:         description="Free-form context for evaluators (not scored).",
  76:     )
  77: 
  78:     @field_validator("question_id")
  79:     @classmethod
  80:     def _id_no_path_chars(cls, v: str) -> str:
  81:         v = v.strip()
  82:         if "/" in v or "\\" in v or ".." in v:
  83:             raise ValueError(
  84:                 "question_id must not contain path separators or '..'"
  85:             )
  86:         return v
  87: 
  88:     @field_validator("expected_pico_axes")
  89:     @classmethod
  90:     def _axes_unique(cls, v: list[str]) -> list[str]:
  91:         if len(set(v)) != len(v):
  92:             raise ValueError("expected_pico_axes must be unique")
  93:         return v
  94: 
  95: 
  96: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  97: # BenchmarkConfig
  98: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  99: 
 100: class BenchmarkConfig(BaseModel):
 101:     """Top-level config for one benchmark run."""
 102: 
 103:     benchmark_id: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=100)
 104:     benchmark_version: str = Field(default="1.0", max_length=20)
 105:     description: str = Field(default="", max_length=1000)
 106:     questions: list[BenchmarkQuestion] = Field(min_length=1, max_length=200)
 107: 
 108:     @field_validator("benchmark_id")
 109:     @classmethod
 110:     def _id_no_path_chars(cls, v: str) -> str:
 111:         v = v.strip()
 112:         if "/" in v or "\\" in v or ".." in v:
 113:             raise ValueError("benchmark_id must not contain path separators")
 114:         return v
 115: 
 116:     @field_validator("questions")
 117:     @classmethod
 118:     def _question_ids_unique(
 119:         cls, v: list[BenchmarkQuestion]
 120:     ) -> list[BenchmarkQuestion]:
 121:         ids = [q.question_id for q in v]
 122:         if len(set(ids)) != len(ids):
 123:             duplicates = sorted({i for i in ids if ids.count(i) > 1})
 124:             raise ValueError(
 125:                 f"benchmark questions must have unique question_id; "
 126:                 f"duplicates: {duplicates}"
 127:             )
 128:         return v
 129: 
 130:     def question_ids(self) -> list[str]:
 131:         return [q.question_id for q in self.questions]
 132: 
 133:     def question_by_id(self, question_id: str) -> BenchmarkQuestion | None:
 134:         for q in self.questions:
 135:             if q.question_id == question_id:
 136:                 return q
 137:         return None
 138: 
 139:     def refusal_bait_count(self) -> int:
 140:         return sum(1 for q in self.questions if q.is_refusal_bait)
 141: 
 142: 
 143: def load_config(path: Path) -> BenchmarkConfig:
 144:     """Load + validate a benchmark config from a JSON file."""
 145:     with Path(path).open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
 146:         data = json.load(f)
 147:     return BenchmarkConfig.model_validate(data)

  91: 
  92: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
  93:     args = parse_args(argv if argv is not None else sys.argv[1:])
  94:     log_level = logging.WARNING
  95:     if args.verbose >= 2:
  96:         log_level = logging.DEBUG
  97:     elif args.verbose >= 1:
  98:         log_level = logging.INFO
  99:     logging.basicConfig(
 100:         level=log_level,
 101:         format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
 102:     )
 103: 
 104:     _LOG.info("loading benchmark config from %s", args.config)
 105:     config = load_config(args.config)
 106:     _LOG.info(
 107:         "config loaded: benchmark_id=%s, %d questions",
 108:         config.benchmark_id, len(config.questions),
 109:     )
 110: 
 111:     if args.skip_polaris:
 112:         _LOG.warning("--skip-polaris set; polaris_results will be empty")
 113:         polaris_results = {}
 114:     else:
 115:         _LOG.info("running POLARIS chain against %s", args.polaris_url)
 116:         polaris_results = run_polaris_against(
 117:             config, args.polaris_url
 118:         )
 119:         succeeded = sum(1 for r in polaris_results.values() if r.succeeded())
 120:         _LOG.info(
 121:             "POLARIS: %d/%d questions succeeded",

 123:         )

 136:         "gemini", args.gemini_dir, config.question_ids()

 140:         len(gemini_report.loaded), len(config.question_ids()),
 141:     )
 142: 
 143:     _LOG.info("scoring %d questions across 7 dimensions", len(config.questions))
 144:     scoreboard = run_benchmark(

 148:         gemini_outputs=gemini_report.loaded,

 170:     print(f"Output: {args.output}")

 172:

 174: if __name__ == "__main__":
 175:     sys.exit(main())

codex
The code confirms an asymmetric design: POLARIS is scored from internal objects and competitors from raw text heuristics, then collapsed into win/tie counts. That is not just a weak scorer; it changes the measurement target from report correctness to artifact-shape recognition.
