 succeeded in 1174ms:
   1: """Report renderer — emit scoreboard.json + report.html + summary.md.
   2: 
   3: Per `.codex/slices/slice_005/architecture_proposal.md` §"report_renderer".
   4: 
   5: Three artifacts produced from a Scoreboard:
   6: 
   7:   scoreboard.json — machine-readable; matches Scoreboard schema
   8:   report.html     — per-question table + aggregate bars (operator demo)
   9:   summary.md      — one-paragraph TL;DR for Carney's office
  10: 
  11: These three files together form the gift's evidence pack for the demo.
  12: """
  13: 
  14: from __future__ import annotations
  15: 
  16: import html
  17: import json
  18: from pathlib import Path
  19: 
  20: from polaris_graph.benchmark.beat_both_scorer import Scoreboard
  21: from polaris_graph.benchmark.dimension_scorers import ALL_DIMENSIONS, DimensionName
  22: 
  23: 
  24: _DIM_LABEL: dict[DimensionName, str] = {
  25:     "sourcing_tier_mix": "Sourcing tier mix",
  26:     "numeric_grounding": "Numeric grounding",
  27:     "provenance_density": "Provenance density",
  28:     "refusal_correctness": "Refusal correctness",
  29:     "coverage_completeness": "Coverage completeness",
  30:     "latency": "Latency",
  31:     "auditability": "Auditability",
  32: }
  33: 
  34: 
  35: def _format_score(v: float | None) -> str:
  36:     if v is None:
  37:         return "N/A"
  38:     return f"{v:.2f}"
  39: 
  40: 
  41: def _format_pct(v: float | None) -> str:
  42:     if v is None:
  43:         return "N/A"
  44:     return f"{v * 100:.0f}%"
  45: 
  46: 
  47: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  48: # scoreboard.json
  49: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  50: 
  51: def render_scoreboard_json(scoreboard: Scoreboard) -> bytes:
  52:     """Canonical JSON serialization (sort_keys=True, UTF-8 bytes)."""
  53:     payload = scoreboard.model_dump(mode="json")
  54:     return json.dumps(
  55:         payload,
  56:         sort_keys=True,
  57:         ensure_ascii=False,
  58:         indent=2,
  59:     ).encode("utf-8")
  60: 
  61: 
  62: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  63: # summary.md
  64: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  65: 
  66: def render_summary_md(scoreboard: Scoreboard) -> str:
  67:     """One-paragraph TL;DR + table of aggregate means."""
  68:     n = scoreboard.aggregate.n_questions
  69:     p_wins = scoreboard.polaris_wins
  70:     e_wins = scoreboard.external_wins
  71:     ties = scoreboard.ties
  72: 
  73:     # Identify POLARIS top-3 dimensions vs best external
  74:     diffs: list[tuple[str, float]] = []
  75:     for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
  76:         p = scoreboard.aggregate.polaris_mean.get(dim)
  77:         c = scoreboard.aggregate.chatgpt_mean.get(dim)
  78:         g = scoreboard.aggregate.gemini_mean.get(dim)
  79:         externals = [v for v in (c, g) if v is not None]
  80:         if p is None or not externals:
  81:             continue
  82:         diffs.append((dim, p - max(externals)))
  83:     diffs.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
  84:     top3 = diffs[:3]
  85: 
  86:     top3_text = (
  87:         ", ".join(
  88:             f"{_DIM_LABEL[dim]} (+{delta:.2f})" for dim, delta in top3
  89:         )
  90:         if top3
  91:         else "no head-to-head dimension data"
  92:     )
  93: 
  94:     md = (
  95:         f"# POLARIS BEAT-BOTH benchmark — {scoreboard.benchmark_id}\n\n"
  96:         f"**Run:** {scoreboard.ran_at_utc.isoformat()}\n\n"
  97:         f"## TL;DR\n\n"
  98:         f"Across {n} clinical questions and 7 dimensions, POLARIS won "
  99:         f"{p_wins} per-question per-dimension comparisons; commercial "
 100:         f"deep-research products won {e_wins}; {ties} ties.\n\n"
 101:         f"POLARIS's strongest dimensions vs. best commercial competitor: "
 102:         f"{top3_text}.\n\n"
 103:         f"## Aggregate means\n\n"
 104:         f"| Dimension | POLARIS | ChatGPT DR | Gemini DR |\n"
 105:         f"|---|---:|---:|---:|\n"
 106:     )
 107:     for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
 108:         p = scoreboard.aggregate.polaris_mean.get(dim)
 109:         c = scoreboard.aggregate.chatgpt_mean.get(dim)
 110:         g = scoreboard.aggregate.gemini_mean.get(dim)
 111:         md += (
 112:             f"| {_DIM_LABEL[dim]} | {_format_score(p)} | "
 113:             f"{_format_score(c)} | {_format_score(g)} |\n"
 114:         )
 115:     return md
 116: 
 117: 
 118: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 119: # report.html
 120: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 121: 
 122: _HTML_HEAD = """<!DOCTYPE html>
 123: <html lang="en">
 124: <head>
 125: <meta charset="utf-8">
 126: <title>POLARIS BEAT-BOTH Scoreboard</title>
 127: <style>
 128:   body { font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; max-width: 1200px;
 129:          margin: 2em auto; padding: 0 1em; color: #222; }
 130:   h1 { border-bottom: 2px solid #333; padding-bottom: 0.2em; }
 131:   .meta { color: #666; font-size: 0.9em; }
 132:   table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; margin: 1em 0; }
 133:   th, td { border: 1px solid #ddd; padding: 0.5em; text-align: left; }
 134:   th { background: #f5f5f5; }
 135:   td.score { text-align: right; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }
 136:   .winner { font-weight: bold; color: #2a7d2a; }
 137:   .loser { color: #888; }
 138:   .na { color: #aaa; font-style: italic; }
 139:   .question-row { background: #fafafa; }
 140:   .system-polaris { background: #e6f4ea; }
 141:   .system-chatgpt { background: #fef7e0; }
 142:   .system-gemini { background: #e8f0fe; }
 143:   .summary-box { background: #f0f0f0; padding: 1em; border-radius: 6px;
 144:                   margin: 1em 0; }
 145: </style>
 146: </head>
 147: <body>
 148: """
 149: 
 150: 
 151: def _highlight_class(score: float | None, peers: list[float | None]) -> str:
 152:     """CSS class for a score cell based on win/lose vs peers."""
 153:     if score is None:
 154:         return "na"
 155:     populated_peers = [p for p in peers if p is not None]
 156:     if not populated_peers:
 157:         return ""
 158:     if score > max(populated_peers):
 159:         return "winner"
 160:     if score < max(populated_peers):
 161:         return "loser"
 162:     return ""
 163: 
 164: 
 165: def render_report_html(scoreboard: Scoreboard) -> str:
 166:     """Operator-friendly HTML scoreboard."""
 167:     out = [_HTML_HEAD]
 168:     out.append(
 169:         f"<h1>POLARIS BEAT-BOTH — {html.escape(scoreboard.benchmark_id)}</h1>"
 170:     )
 171:     out.append(
 172:         f"<p class='meta'>Ran at {html.escape(scoreboard.ran_at_utc.isoformat())} "
 173:         f"&middot; {scoreboard.aggregate.n_questions} questions &middot; "
 174:         f"{len(ALL_DIMENSIONS)} dimensions</p>"
 175:     )
 176: 
 177:     # Summary box
 178:     out.append("<div class='summary-box'>")
 179:     out.append(
 180:         f"<strong>Per-question per-dimension comparisons:</strong> "
 181:         f"POLARIS won <strong>{scoreboard.polaris_wins}</strong>; "
 182:         f"commercial DR products won <strong>{scoreboard.external_wins}</strong>; "
 183:         f"<strong>{scoreboard.ties}</strong> ties."
 184:     )
 185:     out.append("</div>")
 186: 
 187:     # Aggregate table
 188:     out.append("<h2>Aggregate means</h2><table>")
 189:     out.append(
 190:         "<tr><th>Dimension</th><th>POLARIS</th><th>ChatGPT DR</th>"
 191:         "<th>Gemini DR</th></tr>"
 192:     )
 193:     for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
 194:         p = scoreboard.aggregate.polaris_mean.get(dim)
 195:         c = scoreboard.aggregate.chatgpt_mean.get(dim)
 196:         g = scoreboard.aggregate.gemini_mean.get(dim)
 197:         out.append("<tr>")
 198:         out.append(f"<td>{html.escape(_DIM_LABEL[dim])}</td>")
 199:         out.append(
 200:             f"<td class='score {_highlight_class(p, [c, g])}'>{_format_pct(p)}</td>"
 201:         )
 202:         out.append(
 203:             f"<td class='score {_highlight_class(c, [p, g])}'>{_format_pct(c)}</td>"
 204:         )
 205:         out.append(
 206:             f"<td class='score {_highlight_class(g, [p, c])}'>{_format_pct(g)}</td>"
 207:         )
 208:         out.append("</tr>")
 209:     out.append("</table>")
 210: 
 211:     # Per-question detail
 212:     out.append("<h2>Per-question scores</h2>")
 213:     for q in scoreboard.per_question:
 214:         bait_marker = " (refusal bait)" if q.is_refusal_bait else ""
 215:         out.append(
 216:             f"<h3>{html.escape(q.question_id)}{html.escape(bait_marker)}</h3>"
 217:         )
 218:         out.append(f"<p><em>{html.escape(q.question_text)}</em></p>")
 219:         out.append("<table>")
 220:         out.append(
 221:             "<tr><th>Dimension</th><th>POLARIS</th><th>ChatGPT DR</th>"
 222:             "<th>Gemini DR</th></tr>"
 223:         )
 224:         for dim in ALL_DIMENSIONS:
 225:             p = q.polaris.by_dimension.get(dim)
 226:             c = q.chatgpt.by_dimension.get(dim)
 227:             g = q.gemini.by_dimension.get(dim)
 228:             out.append("<tr>")
 229:             out.append(f"<td>{html.escape(_DIM_LABEL[dim])}</td>")
 230:             out.append(
 231:                 f"<td class='score {_highlight_class(p, [c, g])}'>{_format_pct(p)}</td>"
 232:             )
 233:             out.append(
 234:                 f"<td class='score {_highlight_class(c, [p, g])}'>{_format_pct(c)}</td>"
 235:             )
 236:             out.append(
 237:                 f"<td class='score {_highlight_class(g, [p, c])}'>{_format_pct(g)}</td>"
 238:             )
 239:             out.append("</tr>")
 240:         out.append("</table>")
 241: 
 242:     out.append("</body></html>")
 243:     return "\n".join(out)
 244: 
 245: 
 246: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 247: # Combined renderer
 248: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 249: 
 250: def render_report(scoreboard: Scoreboard, output_dir: Path) -> dict[str, Path]:
 251:     """Write all 3 artifacts to output_dir. Returns {kind: path} mapping."""
 252:     output_dir = Path(output_dir)
 253:     output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 254: 
 255:     files: dict[str, Path] = {}
 256: 
 257:     json_path = output_dir / "scoreboard.json"
 258:     json_path.write_bytes(render_scoreboard_json(scoreboard))
 259:     files["scoreboard.json"] = json_path
 260: 
 261:     md_path = output_dir / "summary.md"
 262:     md_path.write_text(render_summary_md(scoreboard), encoding="utf-8")
 263:     files["summary.md"] = md_path
 264: 
 265:     html_path = output_dir / "report.html"
 266:     html_path.write_text(render_report_html(scoreboard), encoding="utf-8")
 267:     files["report.html"] = html_path
 268: 
 269:     return files

   7: Input shape: three accepted inputs.
   8: 
   9:   --verified-sentences <jsonl>   Each line is {"sentence": "...with [#ev:...] tokens..."}.
  10:                                   This is the canonical PRE-RESOLUTION artifact
  11:                                   emitted by clinical_generator/strict_verify.
  12: 
  13:   --report <md> --pool <json>    Legacy / direct text path — assumes report
  14:                                   contains [#ev:...] tokens (works for
  15:                                   INTERNAL test artifacts but NOT for
  16:                                   delivered reports).
  17: 
  18:   --resolved-report <md> --bibliography <json> --pool <json>
  19:                                   I-audit-001: delivered post-resolution
  20:                                   report.md (with [N] citation markers,
  21:                                   tokens stripped) + bibliography.json
  22:                                   ({num → evidence_id}) + pool. The loader
  23:                                   inverts the [N] → evidence_id mapping
  24:                                   and synthesizes provenance tokens against
  25:                                   the entire pool entry's normalized text.
  26:                                   Coarser than token-bearing path (no
  27:                                   per-sentence span boundaries) but enables
  28:                                   audit of real production output for
  29:                                   BEAT-BOTH head-to-head evaluation.
  30:                                   Synthesis layers (Per-Trial Summaries,
  31:                                   Analyst Synthesis) are explicitly
  32:                                   excluded — they have different source-
  33:                                   text contracts and need a separate
  34:                                   audit lane.
  35: 
  36: Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1: BOTH inputs require evidence_pool.json with
  37: the corresponding source spans. Output: per-claim VERIFIED /
  38: PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE verdicts.
  39: 
  40:     VERIFIED   — sentence is fully supported by its cited span(s)
  41:     PARTIAL    — some claims supported, some not; partial entailment
  42:     UNSUPPORTED — content overlap insufficient; cited span doesn't
  43:                   back the claim
  44:     FABRICATED  — sentence asserts content not in any cited span
  45:                   (numeric mismatch, named-entity inflation, etc.)
  46:     UNREACHABLE — span pointer invalid; cannot verify
  47: 
  48: This is the ONLY acceptable evaluation framework per CLAUDE.md §-1.1
  49: (STRICTLY BANNED: word counts, citation counts, pattern presence,
  50: sample-based audits, string-presence checks, metadata comparison).
  51: 
  52: Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1, both Claude AND Codex run independent line-by-
  53: line audits in parallel. This script is Claude's automated audit;
  54: Codex's audit is a separate manual pass via `codex exec`.
  55: 
  56: Usage:
  57:     python scripts/run_line_by_line_audit.py \\
  58:         --report outputs/I-live-1/clinical/.../report.md \\
  59:         --pool outputs/I-live-1/clinical/.../evidence_pool.json \\
  60:         --output outputs/audits/<run-id>/claude_audit.json
  61: 
  62: The model bakeoff (Path A) is a wrapper that runs the pipeline with
  63: multiple --model arguments, then runs THIS audit on each output and
  64: compares the per-claim verdict distribution. That wrapper is
  65: out-of-scope here; this PR ships the foundational audit primitive.
  66: """
  67: 
  68: from __future__ import annotations
  69: 
  70: import argparse
  71: import json
  72: import re
  73: import sys
  74: from pathlib import Path
  75: from typing import Any
  76: 
  77: REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
  78: if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
  79:     sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
  80: 
  81: 
  82: # Reuse strict_verify primitives (mechanical checks (a)-(e))
  83: _DECIMAL_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?")
  84: _WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_\-]+")
  85: _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:([^:]+):(\d+)-(\d+)\]")
  86: # I-audit-001 resolved-report mode: matches "[N]" citation markers in
  87: # delivered post-resolution reports (produced by
  88: # resolve_provenance_to_citations in provenance_generator.py).
  89: _RESOLVED_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[(\d+)\]")
  90: _H2_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^##\s+(.+?)\s*$")
  91: _DEEP_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^#{3,}\s")
  92: _TITLE_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^#\s")
  93: 
  94: # I-audit-001 resolved-mode scope rules (per brief design step 2,
  95: # iter-3 P1-1 fix). Heading text matched case-insensitively after the
  96: # "## " prefix.
  97: _TERMINAL_H2_HEADINGS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
  98:     "methods",
  99:     "contradiction disclosures",
 100:     "bibliography",
 101:     "v30 phase-1 retrieval coverage disclosure",
 102: })
 103: # Synthesis layers use different source-text contracts
 104: # (_m42b_refetched_quote etc.) and need a separate audit lane. Excluded
 105: # from THIS audit's scope; recorded in manifest for visibility.
 106: _EXCLUDED_SYNTHESIS_H2_HEADINGS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
 107:     "per-trial summaries",
 108:     "analyst synthesis",
 109: })
 110: 
 111: _STOPWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
 112:     "a", "an", "the", "and", "or", "but", "if", "of", "in", "on", "at",
 113:     "to", "for", "with", "as", "by", "from", "into", "through", "during",
 114:     "before", "after", "above", "below", "between", "is", "are", "was",
 115:     "were", "be", "been", "being", "have", "has", "had", "having", "do",
 116:     "does", "did", "doing", "will", "would", "should", "could", "may",
 117:     "might", "must", "can", "this", "that", "these", "those", "it",
 118:     "its", "their", "there", "they", "them", "we", "us", "our", "you",
 119:     "your", "i", "me", "my", "he", "she", "his", "her",
 120: })
 121: 
 122: 
 123: def _normalize_pool(raw: Any) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
 124:     """Normalize evidence pool JSON to {id: {evidence_id, direct_quote/full_text/snippet}}.
 125: 
 126:     Accepts:
 127:     - List of {evidence_id, direct_quote/full_text/snippet} (legacy clinical_generator schema).
 128:     - List of {source_id, full_text/snippet} (canonical clinical_retrieval.EvidencePool schema).
 129:     - Object {<id>: <entry>} (already keyed).
 130:     - Object {sources: [<entry>, ...]} (EvidencePool serialized with sources field).
 131:     """
 132:     if isinstance(raw, dict) and "sources" in raw and isinstance(raw["sources"], list):
 133:         raw = raw["sources"]
 134:     if isinstance(raw, list):
 135:         normalized: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
 136:         for entry in raw:
 137:             if not isinstance(entry, dict):
 138:                 continue
 139:             ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id") or entry.get("source_id")
 140:             if not ev_id:
 141:                 continue
 142:             direct_quote = (
 143:                 entry.get("direct_quote")
 144:                 or entry.get("full_text")
 145:                 or entry.get("snippet", "")
 146:                 or ""
 147:             )
 148:             normalized[ev_id] = {
 149:                 "evidence_id": ev_id,
 150:                 "direct_quote": direct_quote,
 151:                 **{k: v for k, v in entry.items() if k not in {"evidence_id", "source_id", "direct_quote"}},
 152:             }
 153:         return normalized
 154:     if isinstance(raw, dict):
 155:         # Already keyed: ensure each entry has direct_quote alias
 156:         normalized = {}
 157:         for ev_id, entry in raw.items():
 158:             if not isinstance(entry, dict):
 159:                 continue
 160:             direct_quote = (
 161:                 entry.get("direct_quote")
 162:                 or entry.get("full_text")
 163:                 or entry.get("snippet", "")
 164:                 or ""
 165:             )
 166:             normalized[ev_id] = {**entry, "direct_quote": direct_quote, "evidence_id": ev_id}
 167:         return normalized
 168:     raise ValueError(f"unrecognized pool shape: {type(raw).__name__}")
 169: 
 170: 
 171: def _normalize_sentence(entry: Any) -> str:
 172:     """Normalize verified-sentence record to string. Accepts:
 173:     - {sentence: "..."}
 174:     - {sentence_text: "..."} (canonical VerifiedSentence schema)
 175:     - "..." (raw string)
 176:     """
 177:     if isinstance(entry, str):
 178:         return entry
 179:     if isinstance(entry, dict):
 180:         return entry.get("sentence") or entry.get("sentence_text") or ""

   1: """I-bench-002 — Paid sample evaluator scoring harness.
   2: 
   3: Wires a paid Layer-3 evaluator (e.g., GPT-5-mini, Opus 4.7, Gemini 2.5
   4: Pro) into the benchmark proof-package per Carney delivery plan v6.2:
   5: external evaluator scores POLARIS report against the 5-question
   6: Carney goldset using PRISMA 2020 / AMSTAR-2 / GRADE per claim.
   7: 
   8: Per CLAUDE.md §-1.1: this is a SECOND independent line-by-line audit
   9: on top of Claude's automated audit (I-bakeoff-A-001). The paid
  10: evaluator is procured by the user; this harness wires the evaluator
  11: output (per-claim verdict JSONL or scoring rubric YAML) into the
  12: benchmark manifest format used downstream.
  13: 
  14: Status: SCAFFOLD. Evaluator procurement is user-action-blocked
  15: (Carney v6.2 Phase 0 Task 0.1). When user provides credentials +
  16: endpoint, the --live flag invokes the real evaluator; default mode
  17: is dry-run that documents the integration shape without spending.
  18: 
  19: Usage:
  20:     # Dry-run: load goldset + report, emit stub manifest
  21:     python scripts/run_paid_evaluator_scoring.py \\
  22:         --goldset config/benchmark/carney_goldset.jsonl \\
  23:         --report outputs/<run>/report.md \\
  24:         --pool outputs/<run>/evidence_pool.json \\
  25:         --output outputs/I-bench-002/scoring.json
  26: 
  27:     # Live: pass --evaluator-endpoint + --evaluator-api-key
  28:     python scripts/run_paid_evaluator_scoring.py ... \\
  29:         --evaluator-endpoint https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions \\
  30:         --evaluator-model gpt-5-mini \\
  31:         --evaluator-api-key $OPENAI_API_KEY \\
  32:         --live
  33: """
  34: 
  35: from __future__ import annotations
  36: 
  37: import argparse
  38: import json
  39: import sys
  40: from pathlib import Path
  41: from typing import Any
  42: 
  43: REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
  44: if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
  45:     sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
  46: 
  47: 
  48: # Default scoring rubric per CLAUDE.md §-1.1 — the FIVE per-claim
  49: # verdicts the paid evaluator must produce.
  50: RUBRIC_VERDICTS: tuple[str, ...] = (
  51:     "VERIFIED",
  52:     "PARTIAL",
  53:     "UNSUPPORTED",
  54:     "FABRICATED",
  55:     "UNREACHABLE",
  56: )
  57: 
  58: 
  59: def build_evaluator_prompt(
  60:     sentence: str,
  61:     span: str,
  62:     framework: str = "GRADE",
  63: ) -> str:
  64:     """Construct the evaluator-facing prompt per CLAUDE.md §-1.1.
  65: 
  66:     The paid evaluator (e.g., GPT-5, Opus 4.7) reads:
  67:       - the sentence + cited span quote
  68:       - the rubric framework (PRISMA 2020 / AMSTAR-2 / GRADE)
  69:     and returns ONE of the five verdicts in RUBRIC_VERDICTS.
  70:     """
  71:     return f"""You are a clinical-research evaluator. Apply the {framework} framework to assess whether the SENTENCE is supported by the CITED SPAN.
  72: 
  73: Return ONE verdict from this list:
  74: - VERIFIED: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is supported by the CITED SPAN. Conservative paraphrase allowed.
  75: - PARTIAL: some claims supported, some are not. The sentence introduces facts beyond what the span supports.
  76: - UNSUPPORTED: the cited span does not back the sentence's claims at all.
  77: - FABRICATED: the sentence asserts content not in any cited span — numeric inflation, named-entity invention, or contradicted by the span.
  78: - UNREACHABLE: the citation pointer is broken (unknown source, span out-of-bound).
  79: 
  80: Return STRICT JSON only:
  81: {{"verdict": "VERIFIED" | "PARTIAL" | "UNSUPPORTED" | "FABRICATED" | "UNREACHABLE", "rationale": "<one short sentence with specific evidence>"}}
  82: 
  83: CITED SPAN:
  84: {span}
  85: 
  86: SENTENCE:
  87: {sentence}
  88: 
  89: JSON:"""
  90: 
  91: 
  92: def score_claim_dry_run(sentence: str, span: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
  93:     """Stub scoring (no LLM call). Returns a 'pending' placeholder."""
  94:     return {
  95:         "sentence": sentence[:200],
  96:         "span_preview": span[:200],
  97:         "verdict": "PENDING",
  98:         "rationale": "dry-run: live evaluator not invoked",
  99:     }
 100: 
 101: 
 102: def score_claim_live(
 103:     sentence: str,
 104:     span: str,
 105:     *,
 106:     endpoint: str,
 107:     api_key: str,
 108:     model: str,
 109:     framework: str = "GRADE",
 110: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 111:     """Invoke the paid evaluator on a single (sentence, span) pair.
 112: 
 113:     Uses httpx synchronously. The endpoint is OpenAI-compatible
 114:     chat-completions; adjust if Anthropic / Gemini.
 115:     """
 116:     import httpx
 117: 
 118:     prompt = build_evaluator_prompt(sentence, span, framework=framework)
 119:     response = httpx.post(
 120:         endpoint,
 121:         headers={
 122:             "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",
 123:             "Content-Type": "application/json",
 124:         },
 125:         json={
 126:             "model": model,
 127:             "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
 128:             "temperature": 0.0,
 129:             "max_tokens": 200,
 130:             "response_format": {"type": "json_object"},
 131:         },
 132:         timeout=60.0,
 133:     )
 134:     response.raise_for_status()
 135:     data = response.json()
 136:     content = data["choices"][0]["message"]["content"]
 137:     parsed = json.loads(content)
 138:     verdict = str(parsed.get("verdict", "")).upper().strip()
 139:     if verdict not in RUBRIC_VERDICTS:
 140:         verdict = "UNREACHABLE"
 141:     return {
 142:         "sentence": sentence[:200],
 143:         "span_preview": span[:200],
 144:         "verdict": verdict,
 145:         "rationale": str(parsed.get("rationale", ""))[:200],
 146:         "model": model,
 147:         "framework": framework,
 148:     }

 176:         sentence = entry.get("sentence", "")
 177:         span = entry.get("span", "")
 178:         broken_pointers = entry.get("broken_pointers", "")
 179:         if not sentence:
 180:             continue

   1: """I-bench-001 — v6 benchmark proof-package harness. Deterministic internal scoring per docs/benchmark/scoring_rubric.md. Always emits all 6 v6 canonical dimensions; suite.score_dimensions filters composite (others get raw=0.0)."""
   2: 
   3: from __future__ import annotations
   4: 
   5: import argparse
   6: import json
   7: import sys
   8: from pathlib import Path
   9: 
  10: sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "src"))
  11: 
  12: from polaris_v6.benchmark.coverage_scorer import score_response_coverage
  13: from polaris_v6.benchmark.schema import (
  14:     BenchmarkScore, BenchmarkSuiteDesign, CompetingSystem, DimensionScore,
  15:     ScoreDimension, SystemAnswer,
  16: )
  17: 
  18: 
  19: _REFUSAL = ("i cannot", "i can't")
  20: _ALL_DIMS: tuple[ScoreDimension, ...] = (
  21:     "factual_accuracy", "citation_health", "frame_coverage",
  22:     "contradiction_handling", "refusal_calibration", "user_traceability",
  23: )
  24: 
  25: 
  26: def _is_refusal(text: str) -> bool:
  27:     low = text.lower()
  28:     return any(m in low for m in _REFUSAL)
  29: 
  30: 
  31: def _score_one(q, sys_id: CompetingSystem, response: str,
  32:                wanted: list[ScoreDimension]) -> BenchmarkScore:
  33:     coverage = score_response_coverage(q, response)
  34:     cit = response.lower().count("http")
  35:     refused = _is_refusal(response)
  36:     pool: dict[ScoreDimension, tuple[float, str]] = {
  37:         "factual_accuracy": (coverage, f"coverage={coverage:.2f}"),
  38:         "citation_health": (min(1.0, cit / 5), f"{cit} citations"),
  39:         "frame_coverage": (coverage, "internal deterministic"),
  40:         "contradiction_handling": (0.5, "internal deterministic"),
  41:         "refusal_calibration": (
  42:             1.0 if refused == bool(q.expected_refusals) else 0.5,
  43:             f"refused={refused} expected={bool(q.expected_refusals)}",
  44:         ),
  45:         "user_traceability": (min(1.0, cit / 3), f"{cit} cites"),
  46:     }
  47:     sel = set(wanted)
  48:     dims = [
  49:         DimensionScore(dimension=d, raw=pool[d][0], rationale=pool[d][1])
  50:         if d in sel
  51:         else DimensionScore(dimension=d, raw=0.0, rationale="not requested by suite")
  52:         for d in _ALL_DIMS
  53:     ]
  54:     raws = [pool[d][0] for d in wanted if d in pool]
  55:     composite = sum(raws) / max(1, len(raws))
  56:     return BenchmarkScore(
  57:         question_id=q.question_id, system=sys_id, dimensions=dims,
  58:         composite=composite, scored_by="automated",
  59:     )
  60: 
  61: 
  62: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
  63:     p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
  64:     p.add_argument("--suite-design", required=True, type=Path)
  65:     p.add_argument("--responses-dir", required=True, type=Path)
  66:     p.add_argument("--output", required=True, type=Path)
  67:     args = p.parse_args(argv)
  68: 
  69:     suite = BenchmarkSuiteDesign.model_validate_json(
  70:         args.suite_design.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
  71:     )
  72:     args.output.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  73: 
  74:     answers, scores = [], []
  75:     for q in suite.questions:
  76:         for sys_id in suite.competing_systems:
  77:             f = args.responses_dir / f"{q.question_id}_{sys_id}.txt"
  78:             text = f.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if f.exists() else ""
  79:             answers.append(SystemAnswer(
  80:                 question_id=q.question_id, system=sys_id, response_text=text,
  81:                 citations_count=text.lower().count("http"), refused=_is_refusal(text),
  82:             ))
  83:             scores.append(_score_one(q, sys_id, text, suite.score_dimensions))
  84: 
  85:     suite_doc = suite.model_dump()
  86:     suite_doc["layer_3_evaluator_required"] = False
  87:     proof = {
  88:         "suite": suite_doc,
  89:         "answers": [a.model_dump() for a in answers],
  90:         "scores": [s.model_dump() for s in scores],
  91:     }
  92:     (args.output / "proof_package.json").write_text(
  93:         json.dumps(proof, indent=2), encoding="utf-8"
  94:     )
  95:     (args.output / "summary.md").write_text(
  96:         f"# Proof package\n\n- {len(suite.questions)} questions\n"
  97:         f"- {len(suite.competing_systems)} systems\n- {len(scores)} scores\n",
  98:         encoding="utf-8",
  99:     )
 100:     return 0

 184: def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
 185:     return {
 186:         m.group(0).lower()
 187:         for m in _WORD_RE.finditer(text)
 188:         if len(m.group(0)) >= 3 and m.group(0).lower() not in _STOPWORDS
 189:     }
 190: 
 191: 
 192: def _decimals(text: str) -> set[str]:
 193:     return {m.group(0) for m in _DECIMAL_RE.finditer(text)}

 195: 
 196: def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
 197:     """Lightweight sentence splitter (matches generator orchestrator)."""
 198:     parts = re.split(r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z\[])|(?<=\])\s+(?=[A-Z])", text.strip())
 199:     return [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]

 201: 
 202: def audit_sentence(
 203:     sentence: str,
 204:     pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 205:     *,
 206:     min_overlap: int = 2,
 207: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 208:     """Per-sentence verdict: VERIFIED / PARTIAL / UNSUPPORTED / FABRICATED / UNREACHABLE.
 209: 
 210:     Mechanical checks (per strict_verify):
 211:     1. provenance tokens present
 212:     2. each token's evidence_id resolvable in pool
 213:     3. span bounds valid against full_text
 214:     4. every decimal in sentence appears in cited spans (numeric_match)
 215:     5. >=N shared content words (overlap_ok)
 216: 
 217:     Verdict mapping:
 218:     - No tokens → UNSUPPORTED
 219:     - Token references unknown source_id → UNREACHABLE
 220:     - Span out-of-bounds → UNREACHABLE
 221:     - decimal mismatch + low overlap → FABRICATED
 222:     - decimal mismatch but overlap ok → PARTIAL (numeric inflation pattern)
 223:     - low overlap but decimals match → PARTIAL (topical inflation)
 224:     - decimals match + overlap ok → VERIFIED
 225:     """
 226:     tokens = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence)
 227:     if not tokens:
 228:         return {
 229:             "sentence": sentence[:200],
 230:             "verdict": "UNSUPPORTED",
 231:             "reason": "no_provenance_token",
 232:             "tokens": [],
 233:         }
 234: 
 235:     span_texts: list[str] = []
 236:     token_dump: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 237:     for ev_id, start_str, end_str in tokens:
 238:         if ev_id not in pool:
 239:             return {
 240:                 "sentence": sentence[:200],
 241:                 "verdict": "UNREACHABLE",
 242:                 "reason": f"unknown_evidence_id:{ev_id}",
 243:                 "tokens": [{
 244:                     "evidence_id": ev_id,
 245:                     "start": int(start_str),
 246:                     "end": int(end_str),
 247:                 }],
 248:             }
 249:         full_text = (
 250:             pool[ev_id].get("direct_quote")
 251:             or pool[ev_id].get("full_text", "")
 252:             or ""
 253:         )
 254:         start = int(start_str)
 255:         end = int(end_str)
 256:         if start < 0 or end > len(full_text) or start > end:
 257:             return {
 258:                 "sentence": sentence[:200],
 259:                 "verdict": "UNREACHABLE",
 260:                 "reason": f"span_out_of_range:{ev_id}:{start}-{end}",
 261:                 "tokens": [{
 262:                     "evidence_id": ev_id,
 263:                     "start": start,
 264:                     "end": end,
 265:                     "max": len(full_text),
 266:                 }],
 267:             }
 268:         span = full_text[start:end]
 269:         span_texts.append(span)
 270:         token_dump.append({
 271:             "evidence_id": ev_id,
 272:             "start": start,
 273:             "end": end,
 274:             "span_chars": len(span),
 275:             # I-bakeoff-A-001 iter-1 P2: include actual span text per
 276:             # CLAUDE.md §-1.1 ("per-claim verdict — with the specific
 277:             # evidence span quote that supports the verdict"). Truncate
 278:             # to 200 chars to bound manifest size.
 279:             "span_text": span[:200],
 280:         })
 281: 
 282:     sentence_clean = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sentence).strip()
 283:     combined_span = " ".join(span_texts)
 284:     sentence_decimals = _decimals(sentence_clean)
 285:     span_decimals = _decimals(combined_span)
 286:     sentence_words = _content_words(sentence_clean)
 287:     span_words = _content_words(combined_span)
 288:     overlap = sentence_words & span_words
 289: 
 290:     decimals_match = sentence_decimals.issubset(span_decimals)
 291:     overlap_ok = len(overlap) >= min_overlap
 292: 
 293:     if decimals_match and overlap_ok:
 294:         verdict = "VERIFIED"
 295:         reason = "all_checks_pass"
 296:     elif decimals_match and not overlap_ok:
 297:         verdict = "PARTIAL"
 298:         reason = f"low_content_overlap:{len(overlap)}<{min_overlap}"
 299:     elif not decimals_match and overlap_ok:
 300:         verdict = "PARTIAL"
 301:         reason = (
 302:             f"numeric_mismatch:sentence={sorted(sentence_decimals)} "
 303:             f"vs span={sorted(span_decimals)}"
 304:         )
 305:     else:
 306:         # Both mechanical checks fail: high confidence fabrication signal
 307:         verdict = "FABRICATED"
 308:         reason = (
 309:             f"numeric_mismatch_AND_low_overlap:sentence_decimals="
 310:             f"{sorted(sentence_decimals)} overlap={len(overlap)}"
 311:         )
 312: 
 313:     return {
 314:         "sentence": sentence_clean[:200],
 315:         "verdict": verdict,
 316:         "reason": reason,
 317:         "tokens": token_dump,
 318:         "decimals_match": decimals_match,
 319:         "content_overlap_count": len(overlap),
 320:     }
 321:

 323: def run_line_by_line_audit(
 324:     report_text: str,
 325:     pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 326:     *,
 327:     min_overlap: int = 2,
 328: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 329:     """Run audit on every sentence in `report_text`. Return summary.
 330: 
 331:     NOTE on verdict semantics (Codex iter-2 P1-1): VERIFIED here means
 332:     "passes mechanical lexical + numeric checks". It does NOT mean
 333:     "the cited span semantically entails the sentence" — that
 334:     requires the LLM-as-judge entailment check (gated by
 335:     `PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT` and run inside strict_verify, not
 336:     here). For Carney audit-grade evaluation, run THIS audit
 337:     (mechanical) AND the entailment judge in parallel; both must
 338:     pass for a sentence to be safe-to-deliver. The audit harness
 339:     reports the mechanical lane; the judge lane is separate.
 340:     """
 341:     sentences = _split_sentences(report_text)
 342:     return _run_audit_on_sentences(sentences, pool, min_overlap=min_overlap)

 344: 
 345: def run_line_by_line_audit_records(
 346:     sentence_records: list[str],
 347:     pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 348:     *,
 349:     min_overlap: int = 2,
 350: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 351:     """Audit a pre-segmented list of sentences (one verdict per record).
 352: 
 353:     Use this when input is JSONL where each line is already one
 354:     verified sentence. Avoids the re-split that
 355:     `run_line_by_line_audit` does on raw report text.
 356:     """
 357:     return _run_audit_on_sentences(
 358:         [s for s in sentence_records if s], pool, min_overlap=min_overlap
 359:     )

 361: 
 362: def _run_audit_on_sentences(
 363:     sentences: list[str],
 364:     pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 365:     *,
 366:     min_overlap: int = 2,
 367: ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 368:     per_sentence: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 369:     counts = {
 370:         "VERIFIED": 0,
 371:         "PARTIAL": 0,
 372:         "UNSUPPORTED": 0,
 373:         "FABRICATED": 0,
 374:         "UNREACHABLE": 0,
 375:     }
 376:     for s in sentences:
 377:         result = audit_sentence(s, pool, min_overlap=min_overlap)
 378:         per_sentence.append(result)
 379:         counts[result["verdict"]] = counts.get(result["verdict"], 0) + 1
 380:     total = len(sentences) or 1
 381:     summary = {
 382:         "total_sentences": len(sentences),
 383:         "verdict_counts": counts,
 384:         "verified_rate": round(counts["VERIFIED"] / total, 4),
 385:         "fabricated_rate": round(counts["FABRICATED"] / total, 4),
 386:         "alert": counts["FABRICATED"] > 0 or counts["UNREACHABLE"] > 0,
 387:     }
 388:     return {
 389:         "milestone": "I-bakeoff-A-001",
 390:         "version": "v1",
 391:         "summary": summary,
 392:         "per_sentence": per_sentence,
 393:         "verdict_semantics_note": (
 394:             "VERIFIED = mechanical (decimals_match + content_overlap >= "
 395:             f"{min_overlap}). Semantic entailment is a SEPARATE check "
 396:             "run via the LLM-as-judge gate; both must pass for safe-to-deliver."
 397:         ),
 398:     }

 401: def _load_bibliography(bibliography_path: Path) -> dict[int, str]:
 402:     """Parse bibliography.json into {num: evidence_id} map.
 403: 
 404:     Bibliography shape (per
 405:     `provenance_generator.resolve_provenance_to_citations` at
 406:     src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:1026-1048):
 407:     list of `{num, evidence_id, url, tier, statement}` dicts.
 408:     """
 409:     raw = json.loads(bibliography_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 410:     if not isinstance(raw, list):
 411:         raise ValueError(
 412:             f"bibliography must be a list, got {type(raw).__name__}"
 413:         )
 414:     bib: dict[int, str] = {}
 415:     for entry in raw:
 416:         if not isinstance(entry, dict):
 417:             continue
 418:         num = entry.get("num")
 419:         ev_id = entry.get("evidence_id")
 420:         if isinstance(num, int) and isinstance(ev_id, str):

 succeeded in 1398ms:
 486: def _load_sentences_with_resolved_citations(
 487:     report_path: Path,
 488:     bibliography_path: Path,
 489:     pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
 490: ) -> tuple[list[str], dict[str, list[str] | bool]]:
 491:     """Read a delivered report.md, invert [N]→evidence_id, synthesize
 492:     `[#ev:...]` tokens so the existing audit_sentence path runs.
 493: 
 494:     Per I-audit-001 brief: the audited span is the ENTIRE normalized
 495:     evidence text selected by `_normalize_pool` (`direct_quote` or
 496:     `full_text` or `snippet`) — no per-sentence boundaries. Coarser
 497:     than the token-bearing path but enables audit of delivered output
 498:     where tokens are stripped.
 499: 
 500:     Returns `(sentences, scope_metadata)` for manifest plumbing.
 501:     """
 502:     report_text = report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
 503:     body_text, scope_metadata = _extract_resolved_claim_body(report_text)
 504:     bib = _load_bibliography(bibliography_path)
 505:     sentences = _split_sentences(body_text)
 506:     synthesized: list[str] = []
 507:     for sentence in sentences:
 508:         # Find every [N] marker; sort by position for stable token order.
 509:         matches = list(_RESOLVED_CITATION_RE.finditer(sentence))
 510:         if not matches:
 511:             # No citation → existing audit_sentence returns UNSUPPORTED
 512:             # ("no_provenance_token"). Keep the sentence so the verdict
 513:             # is visible in the per-sentence dump.
 514:             synthesized.append(sentence)
 515:             continue
 516:         # Strip the [N] markers; append synthesized [#ev:...] tokens.
 517:         stripped = _RESOLVED_CITATION_RE.sub("", sentence).strip()
 518:         tokens: list[str] = []
 519:         for m in matches:
 520:             n = int(m.group(1))
 521:             if n not in bib:
 522:                 # Unresolved citation number — synthesize a token whose
 523:                 # evidence_id is guaranteed missing from the pool so
 524:                 # audit_sentence returns UNREACHABLE with
 525:                 # reason="unknown_evidence_id:__unresolved_<N>__"
 526:                 # (acceptance criterion 4 in the brief).
 527:                 tokens.append(f"[#ev:__unresolved_{n}__:0-0]")
 528:                 continue
 529:             ev_id = bib[n]
 530:             if ev_id not in pool:
 531:                 # I-audit-001 diff iter-2 P2-1 fix: preserve the
 532:                 # canonical `unknown_evidence_id:<ev_id>` diagnostic
 533:                 # when the ev_id is genuinely absent from the pool
 534:                 # (distinct from the present-but-empty-text case
 535:                 # diagnosed as `__empty_text_<ev_id>__`). Synthesize a
 536:                 # token whose ev_id IS the missing id; audit_sentence's
 537:                 # `ev_id not in pool` short-circuit fires with the
 538:                 # exact ev_id in the reason.
 539:                 tokens.append(f"[#ev:{ev_id}:0-0]")
 540:                 continue
 541:             entry = pool[ev_id]
 542:             span_text = entry.get("direct_quote", "")
 543:             if not span_text:
 544:                 # I-audit-001 diff iter-1 P1 fix: pool entry exists but
 545:                 # its normalized evidence text is empty (broken
 546:                 # substrate). MUST fail loudly as UNREACHABLE, not
 547:                 # silently degrade to PARTIAL via the empty-span
 548:                 # content-check path. Route through a distinct sentinel
 549:                 # so the diagnostic reason names the empty-text case.
 550:                 tokens.append(f"[#ev:__empty_text_{ev_id}__:0-0]")
 551:                 continue
 552:             span_len = len(span_text)
 553:             tokens.append(f"[#ev:{ev_id}:0-{span_len}]")
 554:         # Re-attach tokens in stable order. Place after the prose so
 555:         # the existing splitter does not mis-segment.
 556:         synthesized.append(f"{stripped} {''.join(tokens)}")
 557:     return synthesized, scope_metadata
 558: 
 559: 
 560: def main() -> int:
 561:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
 562:         description=__doc__,
 563:         formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
 564:     )
 565:     parser.add_argument(
 566:         "--report",
 567:         type=Path,
 568:         help="Path to a markdown/text artifact containing [#ev:...] tokens. "
 569:              "Mutually exclusive with --verified-sentences and --resolved-report.",
 570:     )
 571:     parser.add_argument(
 572:         "--verified-sentences",
 573:         type=Path,
 574:         help="Path to JSONL file with one verified-sentence per line "
 575:              "({\"sentence\": \"...\"}). Canonical input.",
 576:     )
 577:     parser.add_argument(
 578:         "--resolved-report",
 579:         type=Path,
 580:         help="Path to delivered (post-resolution) report.md with [N] "
 581:              "citation markers. Requires --bibliography. Mutually "
 582:              "exclusive with --report and --verified-sentences.",
 583:     )
 584:     parser.add_argument(
 585:         "--bibliography",
 586:         type=Path,
 587:         help="Path to bibliography.json ({num, evidence_id, url, tier, "
 588:              "statement}). Required iff --resolved-report set.",
 589:     )
 590:     parser.add_argument("--pool", type=Path, required=True)
 591:     parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True,
 592:                         help="Path for the audit JSON manifest.")
 593:     parser.add_argument(
 594:         "--output-md",
 595:         type=Path,
 596:         help="Optional markdown summary ({model}/audit.md format).",
 597:     )
 598:     parser.add_argument("--min-overlap", type=int, default=2)
 599:     args = parser.parse_args()
 600: 
 601:     if not args.pool.exists():
 602:         print(f"ERROR: evidence pool not found: {args.pool}", file=sys.stderr)
 603:         return 1
 604:     modes_set = sum(
 605:         1 for x in (args.report, args.verified_sentences, args.resolved_report)
 606:         if x is not None
 607:     )
 608:     if modes_set > 1:
 609:         print(
 610:             "ERROR: --report, --verified-sentences, --resolved-report are "
 611:             "mutually exclusive",
 612:             file=sys.stderr,
 613:         )
 614:         return 1

 180:             continue
 181:         # I-bench-002 iter-1 diff P1 fix: when any pointer is broken
 182:         # (unknown source_id or out-of-bound span), force UNREACHABLE
 183:         # without invoking the live evaluator. The evaluator can't
 184:         # produce a meaningful verdict on a sentence whose citation
 185:         # is provably broken; spending budget there is wrong.
 186:         if broken_pointers:
 187:             result = {
 188:                 "sentence": sentence[:200],

 203:             result = score_claim_dry_run(sentence, span)

 222:             round(counts.get("VERIFIED", 0) / total, 4) if live else None
 223:         ),

 233: 
 234: def _load_sentences_with_spans(
 235:     report_path: Path,
 236:     pool_path: Path,
 237: ) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
 238:     """Extract (sentence, span) pairs from token-bearing report + pool.
 239: 
 240:     NOTE: this path expects a PRE-RESOLUTION verified-sentences artifact
 241:     that retains `[#ev:id:start-end]` tokens. The DELIVERED `report.md`
 242:     has those tokens stripped (`resolve_provenance_to_citations`) and
 243:     replaced with numbered `[N]` markers — that path returns empty
 244:     spans here. Use `_load_sentences_with_spans_from_jsonl` for the
 245:     canonical pre-resolution input.
 246: 
 247:     Broken pointer preservation: invalid evidence_id or out-of-bound
 248:     span produces an empty span_chars but the sentence is preserved
 249:     in the output stream so downstream UNREACHABLE verdict can still
 250:     fire (the evaluator sees a sentence with no evidence and returns
 251:     UNREACHABLE per rubric).
 252:     """
 253:     from scripts.run_line_by_line_audit import (  # noqa: E402
 254:         _normalize_pool,
 255:         _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE,
 256:         _split_sentences,
 257:     )
 258: 
 259:     pool = _normalize_pool(json.loads(pool_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
 260:     sentences = _split_sentences(report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 261:     pairs: list[dict[str, str]] = []
 262:     for sentence in sentences:
 263:         tokens = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence)
 264:         spans: list[str] = []
 265:         broken_pointers: list[str] = []
 266:         for ev_id, start_str, end_str in tokens:
 267:             if ev_id not in pool:
 268:                 broken_pointers.append(f"unknown:{ev_id}")
 269:                 continue
 270:             full = pool[ev_id].get("direct_quote", "")
 271:             start, end = int(start_str), int(end_str)
 272:             if 0 <= start <= end <= len(full):
 273:                 spans.append(full[start:end])
 274:             else:
 275:                 broken_pointers.append(f"oob:{ev_id}:{start}-{end}")
 276:         clean_sentence = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sentence).strip()
 277:         pairs.append({
 278:             "sentence": clean_sentence,
 279:             "span": " // ".join(spans),
 280:             "broken_pointers": " | ".join(broken_pointers),
 281:         })
 282:     return pairs
 283: 
 284: 
 285: def _load_sentences_with_spans_from_jsonl(
 286:     verified_path: Path,
 287:     pool_path: Path,
 288: ) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
 289:     """Canonical input: JSONL of {sentence_text} or {sentence} per line
 290:     where each sentence retains its [#ev:...] tokens.
 291:     """
 292:     from scripts.run_line_by_line_audit import (  # noqa: E402
 293:         _normalize_pool,
 294:         _normalize_sentence,
 295:         _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE,
 296:     )
 297: 
 298:     pool = _normalize_pool(json.loads(pool_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")))
 299:     pairs: list[dict[str, str]] = []
 300:     for line in verified_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
 301:         line = line.strip()
 302:         if not line:
 303:             continue
 304:         record = json.loads(line)
 305:         sentence = _normalize_sentence(record)
 306:         if not sentence:
 307:             continue
 308:         tokens = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.findall(sentence)
 309:         spans: list[str] = []
 310:         broken_pointers: list[str] = []
 311:         for ev_id, start_str, end_str in tokens:
 312:             if ev_id not in pool:
 313:                 broken_pointers.append(f"unknown:{ev_id}")
 314:                 continue
 315:             full = pool[ev_id].get("direct_quote", "")
 316:             start, end = int(start_str), int(end_str)
 317:             if 0 <= start <= end <= len(full):
 318:                 spans.append(full[start:end])
 319:             else:
 320:                 broken_pointers.append(f"oob:{ev_id}:{start}-{end}")
 321:         clean_sentence = _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sentence).strip()
 322:         pairs.append({
 323:             "sentence": clean_sentence,
 324:             "span": " // ".join(spans),
 325:             "broken_pointers": " | ".join(broken_pointers),
 326:         })
 327:     return pairs
 328: 
 329: 
 330: def main() -> int:
 331:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
 332:         description=__doc__,
 333:         formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
 334:     )
 335:     parser.add_argument("--report", type=Path,
 336:                         help="Path to a token-bearing artifact (NOT delivered report.md). See _load_sentences_with_spans docstring.")
 337:     parser.add_argument("--verified-sentences", type=Path,
 338:                         help="JSONL of {sentence_text} or {sentence}, canonical pre-resolution input.")
 339:     parser.add_argument("--pool", type=Path)
 340:     parser.add_argument("--goldset", type=Path,
 341:                         help="JSONL of {sentence, span} for direct scoring (skip --report+--pool).")
 342:     parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
 343:     parser.add_argument("--live", action="store_true")
 344:     parser.add_argument("--evaluator-endpoint", type=str)
 345:     parser.add_argument("--evaluator-api-key", type=str)
 346:     parser.add_argument("--evaluator-model", type=str)
 347:     parser.add_argument("--framework", type=str, default="GRADE",
 348:                         help="Audit framework: GRADE, PRISMA, AMSTAR-2.")
 349:     args = parser.parse_args()
 350: 
 351:     if args.goldset and args.goldset.exists():
 352:         pairs: list[dict[str, str]] = []
 353:         for line in args.goldset.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
 354:             if line.strip():
 355:                 pairs.append(json.loads(line))
 356:     elif args.verified_sentences and args.pool:
 357:         if not args.verified_sentences.exists() or not args.pool.exists():
 358:             print("ERROR: verified-sentences or pool path not found", file=sys.stderr)
 359:             return 1
 360:         pairs = _load_sentences_with_spans_from_jsonl(

state\compare_gemini_q5.md:111:One of the most significant ancillary benefits of Quebec's universal mandate has been the generation of comprehensive, population-wide data, facilitating highly advanced chronic disease surveillance. The Quebec Integrated Chronic Disease Surveillance System (QICDSS), developed and managed by the Bureau d'information et d'études en santé des populations (BIESP) at the Institut national de santé publique du Québec (INSPQ), stands out as a premier epidemiological tool. Operating without the need for individual consent, it has covered virtually the entire population of Quebec since January 1, 1996, allowing for precise location tracking via postal codes and eliminating the sampling biases inherent in traditional surveys.

state\compare_gemini_q3.md:220:To facilitate seamless, low-risk career transitions, adjustments to Canada’s foundational Employment Insurance framework are critical. Historically, rigid EI rules penalized workers who sought part-time or transitional work while unemployed, clawing back benefits at punitive rates. Recent extensive pilot programs examining "Working-While-on-Claim" (WWC) provisions demonstrated that raising the threshold of allowable employment earnings—or eliminating the threshold entirely while reducing the claw-back rate on all earnings—successfully encouraged claimants to accept part-time or casual roles. Maintaining a foothold in the labour market prevents catastrophic skill atrophy, preserves psychological well-being, and prevents network degradation.
